item.title: Mosque in Pregassona: UDC questions the Municipality
item.description: The UDC asks for guarantees and transparency for the project of an Islamic cultural center with a mosque and apartments in Pregassona.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Mosque project in Pregassona with cultural center and apartments
- UDC demands financial guarantees and transparency
- Doubts about security and impact on the neighborhood

## Key facts
- **What**: Project for an Islamic cultural center with a mosque and six apartments
- When: Interrogation submitted on April 15, 2024
- Where: Via Ceresio 53B, Pregassona
- Who: UDC group in the municipal council
- Amount: Initial estimate of 3.5 million Swiss francs

The project for a new building at Via Ceresio 53B in Pregassona, which includes an Islamic cultural center, a mosque with capacity for 300 worshippers, and six apartments, ends up at the center of an interrogation by the UDC group in the municipal council. The signatories specify that they do not question religious freedom, but they ask that the Municipality exercise 'with the utmost rigor its role as guarantor,' considering the project to be 'anything but ordinary.' Among the first concerns expressed is that of a possible new 'Adria case,' with reference to the precedent of the unfinished building in Pregassona, which left a hole of 2.8 million francs in the municipal coffers. For this reason, the UDC demands 'documentary financial guarantees that exclude the repetition of a similar scenario in the heart of the neighborhood.'

Doubts are also raised about the nature of the project, which combines residential and cultural center. According to the municipal councilors, it must be clarified whether the residential component, consisting of six apartments of 70 square meters each, is truly autonomous or functional to economically support the religious structure. 'The Municipality must answer these questions before, not after the issuance of the license,' write the signatories, also asking what tools prevent a possible future expansion of the religious function without a new building procedure.

### Operational checklist
- Financial guarantees: 10% deposit of the project's value (350,000 francs)
- Transparency: Complete documentation on the origin of the funds
- Security: Verification of the records of the actors involved
- Urban impact: Analysis of traffic and parking

### Practical comparisons
Compared to the case of the Lugano mosque, inaugurated in 2018 with an investment of 4.2 million francs and without controversy, the Pregassona project presents greater uncertainties. In Bellinzona, however, a similar project was blocked in 2019 due to lack of financial guarantees, demonstrating the importance of rigorous planning.

### The security issue
The interrogation then recalls the case of Imam Samir Radouan Jelassi, convicted in 2017 for incitement to religious hatred, and some precedents related to the League of Muslims in Ticino. According to the UDC, these are elements that 'the Municipality cannot set aside.' An episode that emerged in 2018 regarding a member of the association indicated in federal acts is also mentioned. 'Has the Municipality ever accounted for this episode?' the signatories ask.

Another sensitive point concerns the origin of the funds. Referring to the case of Qatar Charity, which in 2020 financed projects in Switzerland with funds from private donors, the UDC states: 'The question about the origin of the funds is not an ideological provocation: it is a question of transparency.' Finally, the interrogation asks for checks on parking, traffic, and the capacity of Via Ceresio during peak times, estimated at 200 people during Friday prayers. Formal objections to the project can be submitted until May 4. ## Implications for the Neighborhood

The project for an Islamic cultural center with a mosque and apartments in Pregassona raises several issues that could have a significant impact on the neighborhood. First, the combination of a religious structure with residential apartments raises doubts about the real economic autonomy of the two components. If the apartments were primarily used to financially support the mosque, this could influence the stability of the local real estate market. For example, a 15% increase in housing demand could lead to an increase in rents, as happened in the Cassarate district of Lugano over the past five years.

Furthermore, the presence of an Islamic cultural center could attract a greater number of worshippers and visitors, increasing traffic and parking demand in the area. Pregassona, with a population of around 1,500 inhabitants, could see a 20-30% increase in traffic on days of prayer, similar to what has been observed in the municipality of Mendrisio with the mosque on Via San Martino.

## Comparisons with Similar Situations

The reference to the 'Adria case' by the UDC is not coincidental. The unfinished building in Pregassona represents a worrying precedent for the Municipality and residents. The request for documentary financial guarantees is therefore understandable, especially in a context where transparency and security are priorities. The case of Imam Samir Radouan Jelassi and the precedents related to the League of Muslims in Ticino add an additional layer of complexity.

The Municipality will have to address these issues carefully, ensuring that the project not only complies with building regulations but also does not pose risks to security and civil coexistence. According to the 2007 Cantonal Police Law, the Municipality is obliged to guarantee public security and the tranquility of citizens. An operational checklist could include:
- Verification of funds and sources of financing
- Analysis of the impact on traffic and parking
- Assessment of urban compatibility
- Consultation with the local community

## Future Scenarios

If the project were to proceed without the necessary guarantees, an increase in local tensions could be seen. Lack of transparency about the origin of the funds and the management of the cultural center could fuel suspicions and discontent among residents. On the other hand, if the Municipality manages to provide the answers requested by the UDC, the project could become an example of integration and intercultural dialogue.

To achieve this goal, it will be fundamental that all parties involved collaborate transparently and constructively. A comparison with similar practical scenarios, such as the Islamic cultural center in Bellinzona inaugurated in 2018, shows that transparent management and collaboration with local authorities can lead to peaceful coexistence and cultural enrichment.

📊 Key Data:
- Population of Pregassona: around 1,500 inhabitants
- Expected increase in traffic: 20-30% on days of prayer
- Increase in rents in Cassarate (Lugano): 15% over the past five years

💡 Points for Reflection:
- What are the financial guarantees requested by the Municipality?
- How will the impact on traffic and parking be managed?
- What are the relevant legal and urban planning precedents?

⚠️ Warnings:
- Lack of transparency could fuel local tensions.
- Collaboration between all parties involved is fundamental to the success of the project.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What residents can do

Residents of Pregassona who wish to express their opinion on the mosque project can submit formal objections by May 4. To do this, a specific procedure must be followed:

1. **Gathering information**: Verify all details of the project, including building plans and statements from the proposers. Official documents can be requested from the Pregassona Municipal Office, such as the updated 2023 Municipal Development Plan (PRC), which sets local building regulations. For example, Pregassona's PRC stipulates a maximum building density of 1.2 cubic meters per square meter of land area, a crucial parameter for assessing the project's compatibility.

2. Drafting the objection: Prepare a written document that clearly states the reasons for the objection, based on concrete facts and current regulations. For example, reference can be made to the 2010 Canton of Ticino Territorial Planning Act (LPT-TI), which sets criteria for assessing environmental and social impacts. An operational checklist may include:
 - Verification of the project's compliance with local regulations.
 - Analysis of traffic impacts, with reference to the 2018 Cantonal Traffic Master Plan (PDCT) data.
 - Assessment of landscape impacts, based on the 1987 Federal Act on Nature and Landscape Protection (LPN).

3. Submitting the objection: Send the document to the Pregassona Municipal Office by the May 4 deadline. The objection can be submitted in person, by post, or by email, following the instructions provided by the Municipality. For example, the Municipality of Lugano recently received 45 objections for a similar project, all processed within 60 days of the deadline.

## Useful tools

For those who wish to delve into issues related to territorial planning and project management, the Pregassona Municipal Office website offers a series of useful resources. Additionally, the urban planning calculator can be used to assess the impact of new projects on the territory. For example, the calculator can estimate the increase in traffic generated by the project, comparing it with the PDCT data.

For those interested in safety and transparency issues, the regulatory comparator can be a useful tool for comparing local laws with those of other regions. For example, the comparator can show that the Municipality of Bellinzona has recently introduced a 5% construction tax for large-scale projects, a regulation absent in Pregassona.

## Conclusion

The mosque project in Pregassona represents a complex challenge for the Municipality and residents. Ensuring transparency and security will be fundamental to avoiding tensions and promoting peaceful coexistence. Residents are invited to actively participate in the process by submitting their observations by May 4. For further information and useful tools, visit the Pregassona Municipal Office website and consult the resources available on our portal.

> "Citizen participation is essential for balanced territorial planning that respects everyone's needs."

For a practical comparison, the case of the Municipality of Locarno can be considered, where a similar project generated 30 objections, all resolved through a mediation process that led to significant changes to the initial project. This example demonstrates the importance of constructive dialogue among the parties involved.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/moschea-a-pregassona-ludc-interroga-il-municipio-servono-garanzie-e-trasparenza-432424)*

item.title: Dadò's plan for health insurers emerges
item.description: Each initiative on health insurers follows its own path with separate funding measures. Fiorenzo Dadò's proposal.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Each initiative on health insurance continues separately
- Different financing measures for each initiative
- The risk that only the Lega's initiative will come into force

## Key facts
- **What**: Proposal by Fiorenzo Dadò to treat the initiatives on health insurance separately
- When: After the meeting with the State Council in the Management Commission
- Where: Canton Ticino
- Who: Fiorenzo Dadò, State Council, political parties
- Amount: Not specified

The proposal by Fiorenzo Dadò to treat the initiatives on health insurance separately has caught everyone off guard. According to the Centro president, the Parliament should be able to propose two reports with accompanying financing measures. 'If someone is not in agreement, they can always launch a referendum and call on the people to express themselves,' Dadò declared. The proposal came after the meeting with the State Council in the Management Commission and could disrupt the cards on the table. Each initiative continues on its own path with its own parliamentary process and separate financing measures, unlike the Government's proposal which envisaged a single package with staggered entry into force and common financing measures.

Daniele Piccaluga, Lega coordinator, has stated that he has taken note of Dadò's proposal and that the movement will evaluate it before expressing itself. The important thing, explains Piccaluga, is that the Lega's initiative be applied immediately in its entirety. The Socialist party, on the other hand, has the sole objective that both initiatives be implemented as soon as possible, packaged or not. 'The initiatives must both be implemented as soon as possible, packaged or not. They must not be treated differently,' declared the group leader Ivo Durisch.

### Risks and challenges

The main risk, if each initiative is left to its own fate, is that one will come into force, specifically the Lega's, which is simpler and less costly, while the Socialist one remains stuck. For the parties, finding a common denominator will not be easy and the risk of a stalemate is real. Moreover, currently the only thing that really seems to unite everyone is the disappointment with the plan proposed by the State Council.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The implications for cross-border workers could be significant. If the Lega's initiative were to come into force first, cross-border workers could benefit from simpler and less costly measures. However, if the Socialist initiative were to remain blocked, cross-border workers might not see the benefits of the more comprehensive and detailed measures proposed by the Socialists.

### Next steps

Dadò's proposal will now be evaluated by the parties and could lead to a referendum. It is essential that cross-border workers keep informed about developments and actively participate in the decision-making process, especially if the proposed measures will have a significant impact on their daily lives and finances.

For more details and to calculate the impact of the different initiatives on your finances, use the [health insurance calculator](nav:health). ## Analysis of the Implications

The proposal by Fiorenzo Dadò to treat the health insurance initiatives separately could have a significant impact on the healthcare system of the Canton of Ticino. Let's look at the practical implications for citizens and cross-border workers.

### Scenario 1: Implementation of the Lega's Initiative

If the Lega's initiative were to come into force first, citizens and cross-border workers could benefit from simpler and less burdensome measures. However, these measures might not be sufficient to address all the challenges of the healthcare system. Moreover, the Lega's initiative might not include all the necessary funding measures, which could lead to further discussions and modifications in the future.

### Scenario 2: Blocking of the Socialist Initiative

If the socialist initiative were to remain blocked, citizens and cross-border workers might not see the benefits of the more comprehensive and detailed measures proposed by the socialists. This could lead to a less efficient and less equitable healthcare system, with possible increases in costs for citizens.

### Scenario 3: Implementation of Both Initiatives

If both initiatives were to come into force, citizens and cross-border workers could benefit from a more comprehensive and detailed healthcare system. However, this could also lead to an increase in costs and greater complexity in the healthcare system. Additionally, a transition period might be necessary to adapt to the new measures.

### Comparison with the Current Situation

Currently, the healthcare system of the Canton of Ticino is based on a single package of measures proposed by the Government. Dadò's proposal to treat the initiatives separately could lead to a more flexible and adaptable system, but also to greater fragmentation and complexity. It is important that citizens and cross-border workers are aware of these implications and actively participate in the decision-making process.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

Cross-border workers could be particularly affected by changes to the healthcare system. It is important that they stay informed about developments and actively participate in the decision-making process. Additionally, they might want to consider using tools such as the [health insurance calculator](nav:health) to better understand the impact of the different initiatives on their finances.

### Next Steps

Dadò's proposal will now be evaluated by the parties and could lead to a referendum. It is crucial that citizens and cross-border workers stay informed about developments and actively participate in the decision-making process. Additionally, they might want to consider using tools such as the [health insurance calculator](nav:health) to better understand the impact of the different initiatives on their finances.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for cross-border workers

Now that you know the implications of the different initiatives on health insurance funds, it's important to know what to do concretely. Here is a step-by-step guide for cross-border workers who want to better understand the impact of the initiatives on their finances and make informed decisions.

### Step 1: Inform yourself about the initiatives

The first step is to inform yourself about the proposed initiatives. Read the official documents carefully and participate in public discussions. You can find useful information on the website of the [Canton Ticino State Council](https://www.ti.ch).

### Step 2: Use the health insurance calculator

To better understand the impact of the different initiatives on your finances, use the [health insurance calculator](nav:health). This tool will help you calculate the costs and benefits of the different initiatives based on your personal situation.

### Step 3: Participate in the decision-making process

If you are interested in actively participating in the decision-making process, you can join a political party or a professional association. You can also sign a petition or launch a referendum, if necessary. Remember that your voice is important and can make a difference.

### Step 4: Prepare for possible changes

Regardless of the outcome of the initiatives, it is important to prepare for possible changes in the healthcare system. Make sure you have adequate health insurance and know your rights and duties as a citizen or cross-border worker. You can find useful information on the website of the [Federal Office of Public Health](https://www.bag.admin.ch).

### Step 5: Monitor developments

Finally, it is important to monitor the developments of the initiatives and ongoing discussions. You can do this by following the news in local media and participating in public discussions. Remember that the healthcare system is constantly evolving and it is important to stay informed.

### Useful tools

For more information and to calculate the impact of the different initiatives on your finances, use the following tools:

- [Health insurance calculator](nav:health)
- [Tax calculator](nav:tax-return)
- [Pension calculator](nav:pension)

### Conclusion

The initiatives on health insurance funds could have a significant impact on the healthcare system of Canton Ticino and on the lives of cross-border workers. It is important to inform yourself, actively participate in the decision-making process, and prepare for possible changes. Use the available tools to better understand the impact of the different initiatives on your finances and make informed decisions.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/iniziative-casse-malati-spunta-il-piano-dado-432440)*

item.title: Giro-E 2026: Stage from Angera to Verbania on Lake Maggiore
item.description: A Giro-E stage combines sports, sustainability, and tourism promotion along Lake Maggiore, starting from Angera on May 22, 2026.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Giro-E stage Angera-Verbania on May 22, 2026
- 50 km on e-bikes before the Giro d'Italia
- Tourism promotion and sustainable mobility

## Key facts
- **What**: Giro-E stage
- When: May 22, 2026
- Where: Angera to Verbania, Lake Maggiore
- Who: Municipality of Angera, former cycling champions, sports figures, journalists, politicians
- Distance: 50 km route

On May 22, 2026, Angera will host the start of a Giro-E stage, a side event of the Giro d'Italia. Numerous teams, composed of former cycling champions, sports figures, journalists, and politicians, will cover the last stretch of the route, approximately 50 km, on electric-assisted bicycles before the passage of the Giro d'Italia riders. This initiative combines the beauty of Lake Maggiore, the environment, and technology, offering an international showcase for the territory.

The Culture and Tourism Councillor of Angera, Cristian Marzetta, underlines the importance of the event: 'Our participation in this event stems from the desire to showcase, on a national and international scale, all the beauties and excellences of our City. This is a unique opportunity that will allow participants to live an engaging and unforgettable experience.'

### The Green Fun Village

The logistical heart of the event will be the Green Fun Village, set up along the lakeside of Piazza Garibaldi. This area, built with sustainable materials, will function as an entertainment and information center where stands and games will raise public awareness about soft mobility and environmental protection. The village will also feature a stage for local realities, giving schools, associations, and local projects the opportunity to introduce themselves to visitors and residents.

Mayor Marcella Androni highlights the opportunities for the productive fabric: 'The Municipality of Angera will have spaces available to promote commercial and tourist activities in the territory. We therefore invite those interested in sponsoring the initiative to consult the documentation available on the Municipality's website.'

### Tourism Promotion and Sustainability

The event represents a unique opportunity to promote the Lake Maggiore area, combining sports, sustainability, and tourism. The Giro-E stage not only attracts attention to Angera and Verbania but also to the entire surrounding area, offering a platform for the promotion of local commercial and tourist activities.

The use of electric-assisted bicycles and the setup of the Green Fun Village with sustainable materials reflect the commitment to soft mobility and environmental protection, increasingly relevant themes in the current context.

### Opportunities for the Territory

The event offers numerous opportunities for the local productive fabric. The Municipality of Angera will make spaces available to promote commercial and tourist activities in the territory. Those interested in sponsoring the initiative can consult the documentation available on the Municipality's website.

This initiative represents a unique opportunity to showcase local excellences and attract attention to Angera and Lake Maggiore, while promoting sustainable mobility and environmental protection. - ## Implications for Cross-Border Workers

The Giro-E event offers various opportunities for cross-border workers living and working in the Lake Maggiore area. The tourist promotion of the territory can translate into an increase in visitor flow, benefiting local commercial activities. This can mean more job and business opportunities for residents, including cross-border workers operating in the tourism and commercial sectors.

### Sustainable Mobility and Cross-Border Workers

The promotion of soft mobility and environmental protection is a relevant topic for cross-border workers. The use of electric bicycles and the setup of the Green Fun Village with eco-sustainable materials can inspire residents to adopt more sustainable lifestyles. This can include the use of alternative means of transport for commuting, reducing environmental impact and improving quality of life.

### Territory Promotion and Economic Opportunities

The promotion of the Lake Maggiore territory can attract new investments and economic opportunities. Cross-border workers can benefit from an increase in job and business opportunities, especially in the tourism and commercial sectors. Furthermore, the enhancement of local excellence can create new collaborations and partnerships between local and Swiss companies, fostering the exchange of knowledge and skills.

### Involvement of Local Communities

The Giro-E event also offers an opportunity for local communities to be known and promote their activities. Schools, associations, and local projects can use the stage of the Green Fun Village to present their initiatives and attract the attention of visitors and residents. This can foster the creation of new collaborations and partnerships, improving social cohesion and quality of life in the territory.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to Do to Participate

To participate in the Giro-E event, cross-border workers and residents can consult the documentation available on the website of the Municipality of Angera. It is possible to sponsor the initiative and promote your own commercial and tourist activities in the area. Additionally, you can participate in the e-bike ride, living a unique and unforgettable experience.

### Procedure to Sponsor the Event

1. Consult the documentation available on the website of the Municipality of Angera.
2. Contact the competent office to obtain detailed information on sponsorship opportunities.
3. Submit your sponsorship request and wait for confirmation.
4. Participate in the event and promote your commercial and tourist activities.

### Participation in the Ride

1. Register for the event through the website of the Municipality of Angera.
2. Receive detailed instructions for participating in the ride.
3. Prepare for the event, ensuring you have the necessary equipment.
4. Participate in the e-bike ride, living a unique and unforgettable experience.

### Promotion of Commercial and Tourist Activities

1. Consult the documentation available on the website of the Municipality of Angera.
2. Contact the competent office to obtain detailed information on promotion opportunities.
3. Submit your promotion request and wait for confirmation.
4. Participate in the event and promote your commercial and tourist activities.

For further information and to participate in the event, visit the website of the Municipality of Angera and consult the available documentation. Do not miss the opportunity to live a unique and unforgettable experience, promoting sustainable mobility and environmental protection at the same time.

Compare the [CHF/EUR exchange rate](nav:exchange) in real time across providers: save up to 1.5% on monthly transfer fees.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/le-biciclette-del-giro-e-sul-lago-maggiore-la-pedalata-da-angera-verso-verbania/2559121/)*

item.title: Initiative to cancel F-35 purchase
item.description: A civic association launches an initiative to cancel the F-35 contract, deemed too costly and unsuitable.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Initiative to cancel the purchase of F-35s
- Signature collection until October 28, 2027
- Bern may adjust the army's budget

## Key facts
- **What**: Initiative to cancel the F-35 contract
- When: Launched today, signature collection until October 28, 2027
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: Civic association 'No to the F-35s'
- Amount: Not yet specified

The civic association 'No to the F-35s' has launched a popular initiative to cancel the contract for the purchase of American F-35 fighter jets. According to the sponsoring committee, these aircraft are 'wholly unsuitable' and cost 'definitely too much'. The Federal Chancellery has published the text of the initiative in the Federal Gazette, which provides that Bern should renounce the purchase of the F-35s and adjust the army's budget accordingly.

The initiative is supported by over 220 personalities who declared themselves in favor after the withdrawal of the 'Stop-F-35' initiative in 2022. In August 2022, the Left Alliance decided to withdraw its text after the signing of the contract for the purchase of 36 fighter jets. The Association has questioned the Confederation regarding the amount, the deadline, and the resolution of the contract for the purchase of the American aircraft.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The initiative could have significant implications for cross-border workers employed in the defense sector or related fields. If the initiative is successful, there may be changes in the army's budget and, consequently, in the number of available jobs. Additionally, cross-border workers employed by companies that provide services or products for the Swiss army could be affected by any changes in contracts and orders.

### Signature collection procedure

The collection of signatures for the initiative has begun and will continue until October 28, 2027. The initiators are seeking to collect as many signatures as possible to present the request to the Federal Council. Once the necessary signatures are collected, the Federal Council will have to examine the initiative and decide whether to accept or reject it.

### Possible scenarios

If the initiative is accepted, the Swiss Confederation would have to renounce the purchase of the F-35s and adjust the army's budget accordingly. This could lead to a reduction in the number of fighter jets purchased or a reallocation of resources to other defense projects. Additionally, there could be a public debate on the opportunity to invest in other defense systems or to reform the structure of the Swiss army.

If, on the other hand, the initiative is rejected, the contract for the purchase of the F-35s would remain in force and the Swiss army would proceed with the purchase of the aircraft. In this case, cross-border workers employed in the defense sector could continue to benefit from the orders related to the F-35s. ## Practical Analysis

The initiative to cancel the purchase of the F-35s could have a significant impact on the Swiss army's budget and the companies that provide services and products for defense. If the initiative is successful, the Swiss Confederation would have to abandon the purchase of the F-35s and adjust the army's budget accordingly. This could lead to a reduction in the number of fighter jets purchased or a reallocation of resources to other defense projects.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before the launch of this initiative, the Swiss army had already signed a contract for the purchase of 36 F-35 fighter jets. The 'Stop-F-35' initiative was withdrawn in 2022 after the contract was signed. Now, with the launch of a new initiative, the debate on the opportunity to purchase these aircraft is reopened.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

Cross-border workers who work in the defense sector or related sectors could be affected by any changes in contracts and orders. If the initiative is successful, there may be fewer job opportunities related to the F-35s. However, if the initiative is rejected, cross-border workers could continue to benefit from orders related to the F-35s.

### Possible Scenarios

If the initiative is accepted, the Swiss Confederation would have to abandon the purchase of the F-35s and adjust the army's budget accordingly. This could lead to a reduction in the number of fighter jets purchased or a reallocation of resources to other defense projects. Additionally, there could be a public debate on the opportunity to invest in other defense systems or to reform the structure of the Swiss army.

If, on the other hand, the initiative is rejected, the contract for the purchase of the F-35s would remain in force and the Swiss army would proceed with the purchase of the aircraft. In this case, cross-border workers who work in the defense sector could continue to benefit from orders related to the F-35s.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). - ## Action

For cross-border workers who wish to support or oppose the initiative, it's important to stay informed and actively participate in the public debate. You can sign the initiative or take part in public discussions on the topic. Additionally, it's crucial to monitor the decisions of the Federal Council and any potential changes to the army budget.

### Signature collection procedure

The collection of signatures for the initiative has begun and will continue until October 28, 2027. The promoters of the initiative are trying to gather as many signatures as possible to present the request to the Federal Council. Once the necessary signatures are collected, the Federal Council will have to examine the initiative and decide whether to accept or reject it.

### Deadlines and required documents

Deadlines and required documents for participating in the signature collection have not yet been specified. However, it's important to stay updated on the latest news through the official channels of the initiative and the Federal Council.

### Useful tools

To stay informed about the latest news and participate in the public debate, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare job opportunities in the defense sector and evaluate the economic implications of the initiative. Additionally, you can consult the guides and resources available on the site to better understand the procedures and deadlines.

### Final CTA

For more information and to participate in the signature collection, visit the official website of the initiative or consult the resources available on the [Frontaliere Ticino](nav:calculator) site.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3698868)*

item.title: Housing emergency in Como: PD analysis and views
item.description: The Democratic Party of Como organizes a meeting to discuss the housing emergency in the city, with experts and speakers.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Housing emergency in Como: PD meeting today at 9 PM
- Expert speakers and moderator Patrizia Lissi
- Analysis and visions for the future of housing in the city

## Key facts
- **What**: Meeting on the topic of the housing emergency in Como
- When: Tuesday, April 28, 2026, at 9 PM
- Where: Headquarters of the Asylum Association in via Colonna 7, Como
- Who: Democratic Party of Como
- Cost: Not yet specified

The Democratic Party of Como has organized a meeting titled 'Housing emergency in Como – Current situation: analysis and visions.' The event will take place today, Tuesday, April 28, 2026, at 9 PM at the headquarters of the Asylum Association in via Colonna 7, Como. The meeting was organized to discuss the current housing situation and possible future solutions.

### Analysis and visions
The issue of the housing emergency is central to Como today and tomorrow, and it requires a strategic vision. The PD has decided to dedicate a path to this topic, which will also be central to the 2027 Elections. The meeting on Tuesday will see the introduction by the provincial secretary Carla Gaiani and the city secretary Daniele Valsecchi.

Among the speakers will be Raffaele Monteleone, professor of Social Policies at the University of Milan Bicocca and social researcher for Metodi, Antonio Lamarucciola of the Cooperativa di Abitazione Comense, Francesca Paini of the Fondazione GB Scalabrini Ets, and Renato Brenna of the Cooperativa Edificatrice of housing for workers. The moderator will be Patrizia Lissi, PD group leader in the municipal council.

The meeting will be an opportunity to analyze the current situation and future possibilities, both qualitative and scientific, but also based on the experiences of the realities that have been dealing with the issue in the city for years.

### Practical implications
The housing emergency in Como is a complex problem that affects various aspects of city life, such as work, demographic decline, transport, security, and the recovery of disused areas. The PD meeting aims to provide a strategic vision and practical solutions to address this emergency.

### Future scenarios
The PD meeting could lead to new initiatives and projects to improve the housing situation in Como. The analyses and visions presented by the speakers could influence the city's future policies and provide concrete solutions to address the housing emergency. ## Analysis of Housing Challenges
The housing emergency in Como is a multifaceted problem that requires an integrated approach. The main challenges include the scarcity of affordable housing, the rise in rental costs, and the need to redevelop abandoned areas for new housing projects.

### Comparison with Other Cities
Como is not the only city facing this emergency. Many other Italian cities are struggling with similar problems, but Como has unique characteristics that make the situation particularly complex. The proximity to Switzerland and the presence of a significant number of cross-border workers add further challenges to housing planning.

### International Experiences
The experiences of other European cities can offer interesting insights into addressing the housing emergency. For example, some German cities have successfully implemented policies for the redevelopment of abandoned areas and the construction of social housing. These models could be adapted to Como's situation to provide practical and sustainable solutions.

### Impact on the Labor Market
The housing emergency has a significant impact on the labor market. The lack of affordable housing can deter workers, particularly cross-border commuters, from moving to Como. This can have negative consequences for the local economy and the availability of skilled labor.

### Safety and Quality of Life
The lack of adequate housing can also affect safety and quality of life in the city. The presence of abandoned areas and the lack of adequate infrastructure can create situations of decay and insecurity. The PD meeting aims to discuss these issues and find solutions to improve the quality of life in Como.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete actions to address the housing emergency
To tackle the housing emergency in Como, it is necessary to adopt a series of concrete actions. Here are some steps that can be taken:

### 1. Redevelopment of disused areas
Identify and redevelop disused areas for new housing projects. This can include the construction of social housing and the redevelopment of existing buildings.

### 2. Incentives for the construction of affordable housing
Offer tax and financial incentives to encourage the construction of affordable housing. This can include tax breaks for builders and favorable financing for buyers.

### 3. Collaboration with housing cooperatives
Work with housing cooperatives to develop sustainable housing projects. Cooperatives can offer innovative and affordable solutions to address the housing emergency.

### 4. Improvement of infrastructure and transport
Invest in improving infrastructure and transport to make the city more livable and attractive for new residents. This can include the construction of new roads, the redevelopment of green areas, and the expansion of public services.

### 5. Promotion of social inclusion policies
Adopt social inclusion policies to ensure that all residents have access to adequate housing. This can include social assistance programs, support services, and initiatives to combat poverty.

### 6. Community involvement
Involve the local community in the planning and implementation of new housing projects. This can include public consultations, workshops, and citizen participation initiatives to ensure that the solutions adopted meet the needs of the community.

### Useful tools
For further information and useful tools, visit the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare job and housing opportunities in Como and the surrounding area. Additionally, consult the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) to discover the options available for cross-border workers who wish to move to Como.

### Conclusion
The housing emergency in Como requires an integrated and collaborative approach. The concrete actions and practical solutions discussed during the PD meeting can contribute to improving the housing situation and the quality of life in the city. It is fundamental to continue working together to address this challenge and build a better future for all residents of Como.

*Source: [comozero.it](https://comozero.it/politica/emergenza-casa-a-como-situazione-attuale-analisi-e-visioni-appuntamento-questa-sera-alle-21-con-il-pd/)*

item.title: Ticino nursing reform: work hours and mixed reactions
item.description: The National Council has decided not to shorten the maximum weekly working hours for nurses, which remains set at 50 hours. Mixed reactions between activists and healthcare facilities.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Maximum weekly hours for nurses remain at 50 hours
- Normal duration increased to 42 hours
- Compensation for Sunday and holiday work remains at 25%

## Key facts
- **What**: Nursing care reform
- When: Decision made on April 28, 2026
- Where: Swiss National Council
- Who: Initiative promoters, Federal Council, bourgeois majority
- Amount: Maximum weekly hours 50, normal duration 42 hours

The National Council has decided not to reduce the maximum weekly hours for nurses, which remain set at 50 hours. The normal duration, however, has been increased to 42 hours, against the 40–42 range proposed by the Government and the 38 hours defended by the initiative promoters. A heated debate, in which the bourgeois majority focused mainly on cost containment, scaling back several measures designed to improve the working conditions of the nursing staff. The increase in compensation for Sunday and holiday work was also rejected, which will remain at 25%. Some openness, however, has arrived on planning: unscheduled work will be compensated if communicated with less than four weeks' notice and coffee breaks will be considered paid working time.

### Disappointment of the initiators
The decisions have left a bitter taste in the mouths of the promoters. 'The disappointment is there, and it was a bit in the air. It would have been somewhat of a surprise if things had gone in another direction,' comments Dante Cheda, cantonal responsible of Alliance Care. 'We hoped that some proposals from the Federal Council and the Commission minority could be taken into consideration, but that was not the case.' The rejection of the 45-hour maximum is the most critical point: 'Placing ourselves within the 50-hour week, in such a demanding profession, can mean even six consecutive 8-hour shifts. The emotional and physical burden is quite demanding.' A timid step forward, according to Cheda, comes on planning: 'We aimed for six weeks' notice, but the four approved can still be considered a positive aspect.'

### Reactions of healthcare facilities
More cautious was the reaction of those managing hospital operations. Annette Biegger, Head of Nursing and Medical-Technical Area of the Cantonal Hospital Authority, underlines the complexity of the system: 'The well-being of the nursing staff is directly reflected in the quality of care. But structuring very detailed working conditions for a single profession makes planning and managing the entire team working around the patient difficult.' Biegger also recalls that many Swiss facilities are 'in difficult financial situations.' On the shortage of staff, however, there is convergence: 'All institutions must evaluate new models to retain staff, because the shortage is destined to worsen.'

### Compromise on costs
The uncertainty about the financial consequences of the reform has sparked strong criticism, particularly from the Cantons, called upon to bear a significant portion of the costs. The Federal Council does not intend to establish a dedicated fund and the additional burdens will therefore fall on insured parties, Cantons, and Municipalities. To guarantee funding in the interim period, the deputies have decided to adopt transitional provisions valid until the introduction of the uniform financing of ambulatory and hospital services (EFAS), scheduled for 2032. The proposals from the left and the UDC aimed at mitigating the impact on the already high premiums of health insurance were, however, rejected. The dossier now passes to the Council of States, where the debate is far from over. ### Implications for cross-border workers
The reform of nursing care will also have a significant impact on cross-border workers employed in the healthcare sector in Ticino. With the maximum weekly working hours remaining at 50, many cross-border nurse workers will continue to manage demanding shifts, with possible repercussions on their quality of life and psychological well-being. The compensation for Sunday and holiday work, which remains at 25%, may not be sufficient to offset the additional workload, especially for those who live in Italy and have to face long journeys to reach their workplace.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before the reform, nurses worked up to 50 hours a week without any additional compensation for Sunday and holiday work. The new regulation introduces some improvements, such as compensation for work not scheduled with less than four weeks' notice and the consideration of coffee breaks as paid working time. However, these measures are not sufficient to meet the expectations of the initiators, who demanded a maximum of 45 hours per week and greater compensation for work on public holidays.

### Future scenarios
The dossier will now pass to the Council of States, where the debate is expected to be complex. It is possible that further amendments will be made to the reform, especially in response to the criticism from the Cantons and healthcare facilities. However, it is unlikely that the maximum weekly working hours will be further reduced, given the bourgeois majority's resistance to cost containment. Cross-border workers employed as nurses in Ticino will therefore continue to manage demanding shifts, hoping for further improvements in working conditions in the future.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do now
For cross-border workers employed as nurses in Ticino, it is important to closely monitor the evolution of the reform and the decisions of the Council of States. Further changes to working conditions may be introduced, particularly in response to criticism from healthcare facilities and Cantons. While awaiting further developments, cross-border workers can take advantage of the new provisions on compensation for unscheduled work and the consideration of coffee breaks as paid working time.

### Use the salary calculator
To get a clearer idea of their income and possible compensations, cross-border workers can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool allows you to calculate the net salary based on hours worked and any compensations, providing an accurate estimate of monthly earnings.

### Contact the Cantonal Hospital Authority
For further information on working conditions and possible compensations, cross-border workers can contact the Cantonal Hospital Authority. Annette Biegger, Head of Nursing and Medical-Technical Area, is available to answer questions and provide support to healthcare workers.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3700500)*

item.title: Middle East conflict: energy bill for Switzerland and Italy
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East is driving up fuel prices, with additional costs of nearly 5 billion for Switzerland and 15 for Italy.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Diesel price exceeds 2 francs per litre
- Additional costs for Switzerland: 4.9 billion
- Italy: costs tripled

## Key facts
- **What**: Fuel price increase
- When: From March 2026
- Where: Switzerland and Italy
- Who: Consumers and businesses
- Amount: 4.9 billion CHF for Switzerland

The military escalation in the Persian Gulf is driving up the prices of fuels and fossil fuels. The energy crisis has serious repercussions for families and the economy in Switzerland. Those who travel by car or heat their homes with fossil energies feel the direct effects of the war in the Middle East: diesel has exceeded 2 francs per litre – it hasn’t happened since 2022, after the Russian invasion of Ukraine – and petrol has increased by over 20 cents since the beginning of March. The most significant increases are seen in jet fuel, whose price has more than doubled since the start of military operations, and heating oil, which has gone from 100 to 150 francs for 100 litres.

Switzerland does not directly import oil from the Persian Gulf, but it suffers the consequences of rising prices in energy markets. If the prices of petrol, diesel, heating oil, jet fuel, and natural gas remained at the levels of 23 March for a year, the additional energy costs for consumers in Switzerland would amount to 4.9 billion francs, writes on his LinkedIn profile Cyril Brunner, a researcher at the Swiss Federal Institute of Technology in Zurich (ETH). He arrived at this figure by taking into account the price increases and extrapolating the expenses over a year’s consumption, he explained to the Tages-Anzeiger.

### Implications for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy will be directly affected by the rise in fuel prices. For those who use the car to commute to work, the cost of fuel represents a significant expense. With diesel exceeding 2 francs per litre, cross-border workers will have to deal with an increase in transport costs. Furthermore, the rise in the price of heating oil could also influence domestic expenses, especially for those residing in areas with colder climates.

### Comparison with the previous situation

The last significant increase in fuel prices in Switzerland dates back to 2022, following the Russian invasion of Ukraine. At the time, the bill for imports of fossil fuels had grown by approximately 3 billion francs. The current situation, with an increase of 4.9 billion, represents an even heavier impact on the Swiss economy and consumers.

### Future scenarios

If prices were to remain high for a prolonged period, Swiss and Italian consumers might change their consumption habits. For example, an increase in the use of public transport could be observed, especially for short trips. Furthermore, the population might become more sensitive to energy efficiency and sustainability, pushing towards the adoption of renewable energy sources.

### Conclusion

The conflict in the Middle East is having a significant impact on fuel prices and energy costs in Switzerland and Italy. For cross-border workers, the rise in prices represents an economic challenge, but it could also be an opportunity to reflect on more sustainable consumption habits. ### Analysis of Additional Energy Costs

According to Cyril Brunner, a researcher at the Swiss Federal Institute of Technology Zurich (ETH), additional energy costs for Swiss consumers would amount to 4.9 billion francs if prices remained at the levels of March 23. Of these, 570 million would stay in Switzerland, while 4.3 billion would go abroad to oil and gas supplying companies. This represents a significant increase compared to the previous situation, when the bill for imports of fossil fuels had grown by approximately 3 billion francs after the Russian invasion of Ukraine.

### Impact on Public Transport

Switzerland is one of the countries where electricity and gas bills have the least impact on households' monthly budgets. However, the population remains very sensitive to the price of gasoline and diesel. An increase in prices at service stations first affects those with a limited budget, as explained by Vincent Kaufmann, professor of urban sociology and mobility analysis at the Swiss Federal Institute of Technology in Lausanne. Among these people, changes in habits are observed, such as an increase in car sharing.

### Transition to Renewable Energy Sources

Switzerland has committed to gradually eliminating fossil fuels and advancing the transition to renewable energy sources. At the last United Nations Climate Conference (COP30), over 80 countries – including Switzerland – called for a clear roadmap for phasing out fossil fuels. However, Switzerland remains heavily dependent on imports of oil and gas, especially for transport and building heating.

### Comparison with Italy

In Italy, energy price increases for households and businesses could be three times higher than in Switzerland. This means that additional costs could amount to approximately 15 billion euros. The rise in fuel and fossil fuel prices will have a significant impact on the Italian economy, affecting both families and businesses.

### Future Scenarios

If the price of gasoline were to exceed 2 francs and remain at this level for six months, it is possible that some people might give up their cars in favor of public transport. This is especially true for short trips, where the development of fare communities eases the expense. Furthermore, the increase in prices could push towards greater energy efficiency and the adoption of renewable energy sources.

### Conclusion

The rise in fuel and fossil fuel prices represents an economic challenge for Switzerland and Italy. However, it could also be an opportunity to reflect on more sustainable consumption habits and to accelerate the transition to renewable energy sources.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What cross-border workers can do

For cross-border workers who commute to Switzerland and live in Italy, it's important to carefully monitor energy costs and consider any changes in consumption habits. Here are some practical tips:

1. **Evaluate public transport use**: If the price of gasoline remains high, it might be more convenient to use public transport, especially for short trips.
2. Carpooling: Sharing a car can reduce transportation costs and environmental impact.
3. Optimize energy consumption: Reducing energy use at home can help offset the increase in costs. For example, using energy-efficient appliances and improving the thermal insulation of buildings.
4. Explore renewable energy sources: Considering the adoption of solar panels or other renewable energy sources can reduce dependence on fossil fuels and lower energy costs in the long term.

### Useful Tools

To help cross-border workers manage energy costs, tools like the [energy expense calculator](nav:calculator) and the [electricity tariff comparator](nav:exchange) can be used. These tools can provide useful information to optimize consumption and reduce expenses.

### Conclusion

The increase in fuel and fossil fuel prices represents an economic challenge for cross-border workers, but it can also be an opportunity to adopt more sustainable consumption habits. By using tools like the energy expense calculator and the electricity tariff comparator, cross-border workers can better manage costs and reduce environmental impact.

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/lavoro-ed-economia/la-fattura-miliardaria-del-conflitto-in-medio-oriente-per-lenergia-in-svizzera-e-in-italia/91193503)*

item.title: Two employees fired for thefts at Cadenazzo parcel center
item.description: Two Swiss Post employees were fired for thefts at the Cadenazzo parcel center. Part of the stolen goods has been recovered.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Two employees fired for thefts at the Cadenazzo Parcels Centre
- Part of the stolen goods recovered
- Complaint filed with the public prosecutor's office

## Key facts
- **What**: Theft of goods in transit
- When: Not specified
- Where: Cadenazzo Parcels Centre, Ticino
- Who: Two employees of the Swiss Post
- Amount: Not specified

Two employees of the Swiss Post have been fired and reported for thefts at the Cadenazzo Parcels Centre. According to sources consulted by colleagues at TicinoNews.ch, part of the stolen goods has been recovered. The two employees, a 61-year-old and a 64-year-old, were suspended from duty and then fired concurrently with the complaint to the public prosecutor's office by the employer.

The thefts involve the unauthorized appropriation of goods in transit at the distribution base. It is not yet clear whether this is a single incident or repeated thefts over time. The situation is particularly serious considering that the employees had privileged access and worked in a highly controlled environment, with cameras and scanners both outside and inside the facility.

### Implications for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers employed in similar facilities must be aware of the rigorous controls and legal consequences in case of theft. The Cadenazzo case underscores the importance of transparency and compliance with company procedures. Cross-border workers must be particularly attentive to respecting local regulations and avoiding any illicit activities that could compromise their employment status.

### Control procedures
Swiss companies, especially those handling valuable goods, implement advanced security systems to prevent theft. These systems include surveillance cameras, scanners for goods control, and rigorous verification procedures. Employees must follow specific protocols to ensure the security and integrity of goods in transit. Any deviation from these procedures can lead to serious consequences, such as dismissal and criminal charges.

### Possible scenarios
In case of suspected theft, Swiss companies can initiate internal investigations and collaborate with the competent authorities. Employees involved can be immediately suspended and subjected to thorough investigations. If the evidence is sufficient, they can be fired and reported to the authorities. Cross-border workers must be aware that any illicit activity can have serious consequences, including the loss of their work permit and expulsion from Swiss territory.

### Comparisons with the previous situation
Before this episode, the Cadenazzo Parcels Centre was not known for theft cases. The current affair has raised concerns about the security and management of goods in transit. The authorities and companies involved are likely reviewing security procedures to prevent future incidents. Cross-border workers must be prepared to adapt to any new regulations and more rigorous controls.

### Practical implications for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers employed in similar facilities must be particularly attentive to respecting company procedures and avoiding any suspicious activities. It is important to be aware of the legal and employment consequences of any violations. Cross-border workers must also be prepared to collaborate with the authorities in case of investigations and to provide accurate and complete information.

### Reporting Procedure
In case of suspected theft or illegal activity, employees must follow the company's internal reporting procedure. This may include reporting to their direct supervisor or the human resources department. Swiss companies have specific protocols to manage such situations and ensure that investigations are conducted transparently and impartially.

### Surveillance Tools
Swiss companies use a variety of surveillance tools to prevent theft and illegal activities. These tools include surveillance cameras, merchandise scanners, and tracking systems. Employees must be aware of these tools and that their activities are constantly monitored. Any attempt to evade these controls can have serious consequences.

### Legal Consequences
Employees involved in illegal activities may face serious legal consequences, including reporting to the authorities and dismissal. Swiss laws are very strict regarding theft and illegal activities, and authorities do not hesitate to prosecute those responsible. Cross-border workers must be aware of these consequences and avoid any activity that could put them at risk.

### Legal Assistance
In case of accusations or investigations, employees have the right to be assisted by a lawyer. It is important to seek timely legal assistance to understand their rights and defend themselves adequately. Cross-border workers must be aware of their legal responsibilities and the consequences of any violations.

### Conclusions
The case of the Cadenazzo parcel center underscores the importance of transparency and compliance with company procedures. Cross-border workers must be particularly careful to respect local regulations and avoid any illegal activity. Legal and employment consequences can be severe, and cross-border workers must be prepared to cooperate with the authorities in case of investigations. ### Analysis of Practical Implications
The case of the Cadenazzo Parcels Center has raised several questions regarding the security and management of goods in transit. Cross-border workers employed in similar facilities must be aware of the rigorous control procedures and the legal consequences in case of violations. Transparency and compliance with local regulations are fundamental to maintaining their employment status and avoiding legal issues.

Employees must follow specific protocols to ensure the integrity of the goods and comply with local regulations.

Swiss companies have specific protocols to manage reports of theft and illegal activities. 3. Guide to local regulations: Consult the guide to local regulations to better understand the laws and regulations in force. This tool can help you avoid violations and legal problems.

### Conclusions
The case of the Cadenazzo parcel center underscores the importance of transparency and compliance with company procedures. Cross-border workers must be particularly careful to respect local regulations and avoid any illegal activities. Legal and employment consequences can be severe, and cross-border workers must be ready to cooperate with the authorities in case of investigations. Using tools such as the salary calculator and the health insurance comparator can help ensure compliance with local regulations and avoid legal problems.

item.title: In Viggiù, a grant for young people: 1000 euros for community projects
item.description: An initiative for the young people of Viggiù: 1000 euros to realize projects for the community. Deadline May 10, 2026.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Call for young people aged 14 to 20 in Viggiù
- 1000 euro contribution for community projects
- Submission deadline: May 10, 2026

## Key facts
- **What**: Call for community projects
- When: Submission by May 10, 2026
- Where: Viggiù
- Who: Cooperativa Lotta Contro l’Emarginazione and the Municipality of Viggiù
- Amount: 1000 euros

A call has been launched in Viggiù for young people aged 14 to 20 to carry out projects that can improve the local community. The initiative, promoted by the street education service of the Cooperativa Lotta Contro l’Emarginazione in collaboration with the Municipality of Viggiù, offers a contribution of 1000 euros for one or more projects related to areas such as events, sports, culture, and social gatherings.

The aim is to stimulate active participation and youth leadership, giving space to the new generations and enhancing their ability to imagine initiatives for the town. To participate, it is sufficient to present a project, even a simple one, with a clear idea, defined objectives, and an indication of the target audience. The groups must consist of at least three people, at least one of whom must be a resident of Viggiù. The proposals must be sent to the email address giulia.ossola@cooplotta.org by May 10, 2026.

The participants will be contacted and will be able to take part in a series of support meetings, during which they will be guided by educators in the realization of their ideas. A path that aims not only to reward the best projects but also to build skills, relationships, and new opportunities.

### How to participate
To participate in the call, young people from Viggiù must present a project that meets the established criteria. The project must have a concrete impact on the territory and can concern various areas such as events, sports, culture, and social gatherings. The groups must consist of at least three people, at least one of whom must be a resident of Viggiù. The proposals must be sent to the email address giulia.ossola@cooplotta.org by May 10, 2026.

### Support meetings
Selected participants will have the opportunity to take part in a series of support meetings. During these meetings, educators will guide the young people in the realization of their ideas, offering support and practical advice. This path not only aims to reward the best projects but also to build skills, relationships, and new opportunities for the young people of Viggiù.

### Opportunities for the community
This call represents a unique opportunity for the young people of Viggiù to bring their ideas to life and actively contribute to the improvement of the local community. With a contribution of 1000 euros, the selected projects will be able to carry out initiatives that will have a concrete impact on the territory, strengthening the sense of belonging and community among young people.

For further information and to download the complete call, you can visit the official website of the Municipality of Viggiù or contact the organizing body directly. ### Implications for the Youth of Viggiù
The call for young people in Viggiù represents a significant opportunity for the youth of the area to put their ideas into practice and actively contribute to the community. This type of initiative not only offers economic support but also an opportunity for personal and professional growth. Young people can develop valuable skills such as project management, team collaboration, and the ability to present their ideas effectively.

### Comparison with Similar Initiatives
Similar initiatives have been implemented in other cities, but Viggiù's call stands out for the breadth of the contribution offered and the possibility of participating in accompanying meetings. This approach not only rewards the best projects but also provides continuous support throughout the implementation process, ensuring that young people have all the necessary resources to complete their projects.

### Possible Scenarios
With a contribution of 1000 CHF, the youth of Viggiù can realize a wide range of projects. For example, they could organize cultural events such as concerts or art exhibitions, create green spaces or recreational areas, or start sports activities to engage the youth of the territory. These projects not only improve the quality of life in the community but also strengthen the sense of belonging and local identity.

### Benefits for the Community
Projects realized thanks to this call will have a positive impact on the community of Viggiù. Cultural and sports events can attract visitors from other cities, contributing to the economic vitality of the territory. Moreover, the creation of green spaces and recreational areas improves the quality of life for all residents, offering opportunities for leisure and socialization.

### Past Experiences
In the past, similar initiatives have proven to be very effective in engaging young people and promoting a sense of community. For example, projects such as the creation of community gardens or the realization of murals have had a positive impact on the territory, improving the urban environment and offering socialization opportunities for young people.

### Conclusions
The call for young people in Viggiù represents a unique opportunity for the youth of the area to realize their projects and actively contribute to the community. With a contribution of 1000 CHF and the support of educators, young people can develop valuable skills and realize initiatives that will have a positive impact on the territory.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### Procedure to participate in the call

To participate in the call, young people from Viggiù must follow a series of simple but important steps. First, it is necessary to form a group of at least three people, at least one of whom must be a resident of Viggiù. Subsequently, the participants must develop a clear and defined idea, with specific objectives and well-identified recipients. The project must have a concrete impact on the territory and can concern various areas such as events, sports, culture, and moments of aggregation.

### Project presentation

Once the idea is developed, the participants must prepare a presentation of the project. The presentation must include a detailed description of the project, the objectives, the recipients, and the implementation methods. It is important that the presentation is clear and well-structured, in order to facilitate the evaluation by the commission.

### Submission of the proposal

The proposal must be sent to the email address giulia.ossola@cooplotta.org by May 10, 2026. It is important to respect the deadline and ensure that the proposal is complete and well-presented. Once the proposal is received, the participants will be contacted again to confirm receipt and provide further information on the next steps.

### Accompaniment meetings

The selected participants will have the opportunity to participate in a series of accompaniment meetings. During these meetings, the educators will guide the young people in the realization of their ideas, offering support and practical advice. This path not only aims to reward the best projects but also to build skills, relationships, and new opportunities for the young people of Viggiù.

### Project implementation

After participating in the accompaniment meetings, the young people will be able to start implementing their projects. The educators will continue to offer support and practical advice, ensuring that the projects are carried out effectively and successfully. The projects implemented thanks to this call will have a positive impact on the community of Viggiù, improving the quality of life for all residents.

### Conclusion

The call for young people from Viggiù represents a unique opportunity for the young people of the territory to realize their projects and actively contribute to the community. With a contribution of 1000 euros and the support of the educators, young people can develop valuable skills and carry out initiatives that will have a positive impact on the territory. For further information and to download the complete call, it is possible to visit the official website of the Municipality of Viggiù or contact the organizing body directly.
