item.title: Podcast on 'A Europe from below': Matteo Bianchi on regional roles
item.description: The vice-president of the Committee of the Regions discusses cohesion policies and participatory governance in the EU.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Podcast on EU policies and the role of regions with Matteo Bianchi
- Distinction between 'top-down' and 'bottom-up' Europe
- Cohesion funds to reduce inequalities

## Key facts
- **What**: Podcast on EU policies and the role of regions
- When: 2026
- Where: Not specified
- Who: Matteo Bianchi, Massimo Gaudina, Vincenzo Salvatore
- Amount: Not specified

In the podcast episode “Hands off Europe,” European official Massimo Gaudina, Insubria University professor Vincenzo Salvatore, and Vice President of the Committee of the Regions Matteo Bianchi delved into the role of cohesion policies and the weight of local autonomies in European decisions. The discussion highlighted how Brussels' choices directly impact territories and the daily lives of citizens.

At the heart of the debate is the distinction between a 'top-down' Europe and a 'bottom-up' Europe. In the former, decisions come from central institutions with the goal of standardizing territories. In the latter, the process is built from the bottom up, valuing local specificities. 'We need a subsidiary approach that starts from the territories' – emphasized Matteo Bianchi, Vice President of the Committee of the Regions – highlighting the need to overcome an excessively centralist vision. In this perspective, regions and municipalities become key players in bringing needs and priorities to Brussels.

### The role of cohesion funds

Ample space is dedicated to cohesion policies, considered one of the main tools of the European Union to reduce economic and social inequalities. Structural funds finance infrastructure, support employment, and accompany territories in development processes. For Italy, these are significant resources that in some regions have a substantial impact on public budgets. Not just economic transfers, but strategic levers to rebalance differences between more and less developed areas.

### Participatory governance

The model described by the speakers is one of participatory governance. Regions negotiate directly with the European Commission and contribute to the definition of policies through the Committee of the Regions, a consultative body that expresses opinions on regulations impacting territories. 'European legislation largely concerns the life of local communities' – stated Vincenzo Salvatore, professor at Insubria University – an aspect that makes the involvement of autonomies fundamental.

### Difficulties in spending funds

Criticities are not lacking. Among these, the ability to program and especially to spend the resources. The so-called 'grounding' of the funds remains one of the main issues, with marked differences between Italian regions. A limit that also opens the debate on possible simplifications, but with the risk – highlighted in the discussion – of reducing the role of territories in favor of a more centralized management.

### The value of European solidarity

In conclusion, the speakers recall the solidaristic principle of the European Union. A mechanism that cannot be read only in terms of balance between what a State contributes and what it receives, but as a common investment to guarantee stability and development in the long term.

The appointment with Gaudina and Salvatore on Materia is scheduled for May 12 at 9 PM. The process of European integration, from the origins of the Schuman Declaration and the Ventotene Manifesto, is today put to the test of history. The European Union is at a crossroads: renew itself or risk decline. There is a question about the possibility of new forms of integration. ### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

Cohesion policies and participatory governance have a significant impact on cross-border workers who commute to Ticino from Italy. Structural funds can finance infrastructure that improves the quality of life and working conditions, such as more efficient public transport and improved healthcare services. This is particularly relevant for cross-border workers who cross the border daily to reach their workplace.

### Comparison of Governance Models

The 'bottom-up' model promoted by Matteo Bianchi could lead to greater attention to the specific needs of cross-border workers. For example, regions could negotiate with the European Commission to obtain funds aimed at improving border infrastructure, such as the Brogeda and Gaggiolo crossings, making the passage between Italy and Switzerland more fluid. This could reduce waiting times and improve the daily experience of cross-border workers.

### Challenges in Managing Funds

The ability to spend cohesion funds represents a significant challenge. Differences between Italian regions in managing these funds can lead to inequalities. For cross-border workers, this could mean that some regions benefit from better infrastructure and services than others. It is essential that regions improve their programming and spending capacity to ensure that funds are used effectively and benefit all citizens, including cross-border workers.

### The Role of the Committee of the Regions

The Committee of the Regions plays a crucial role in ensuring that European regulations take into account local needs. For cross-border workers, this means that their concerns and needs can be brought to the attention of European institutions. For example, the Committee could express opinions on regulations concerning cross-border work, such as employment conditions and workers' rights, contributing to the creation of a fairer and more favorable working environment.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What cross-border workers can do

For cross-border workers who wish to benefit from cohesion policies and participatory governance, it's important to stay informed and actively participate in local decision-making processes. Here are some concrete steps:

1. **Stay informed about local policies**: Keep up-to-date with initiatives and projects funded by cohesion funds in your regions. This can help you better understand how these policies can impact your daily life.
2. Participate in public consultations: Join public consultations organized by local and regional authorities to express your opinions and needs. This is a way to make your voice heard and influence decisions that affect the community.
3. Contact local representatives: Reach out to local and regional representatives to discuss your concerns and suggest improvements. Representatives can bring these concerns to the Committee of the Regions and help formulate more effective policies.
4. Utilize available funds: Take advantage of available cohesion funds to improve local infrastructure and services. This can include participating in local development projects or applying for funding for initiatives that improve quality of life.

### Useful Tools

For cross-border workers who want to delve deeper into cohesion policies and participatory governance, there are several useful tools available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. For example, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help you better understand the tax implications of European policies. Additionally, the [cost-of-living](nav:cost-of-living) section provides detailed information on living costs in Ticino and neighboring regions, helping cross-border workers plan their finances better.

### Conclusion

Cohesion policies and participatory governance represent an important opportunity to improve the lives of cross-border workers. Actively participating in local decision-making processes and taking advantage of available funds can make a difference. By using the tools available on the Frontaliere Ticino website, cross-border workers can stay informed and make more conscious decisions for their future.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/uneuropa-dal-basso-il-ruolo-delle-regioni-nel-podcast-con-matteo-bianchi/2559955/)*

item.title: Fondazione Xenia: Generational Pact for Disabled Children
item.description: An innovative project to ensure a peaceful future for disabled children, inspired by the tradition of the court house.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Generational Pact project for long-term care
- Host families in exchange for mutual assistance
- Model inspired by the traditional court house

## Key facts
- **What**: Generational Pact for assistance to disabled children
- When: Project currently active
- Where: Fondazione Xenia, Orino
- Who: Fondazione Xenia, participating families
- Amount: Free accommodation for host family

In the new episode of Soci All Time, the podcast by Radio Materia produced in collaboration with Csv Insubria, an truly innovative project that is making the whole community talk was discussed: the “Generational Pact” of the Fondazione Xenia in Orino. This initiative, born from an idea of two parents from Milan, aims to guarantee a serene and inclusive future for people with intellectual disabilities, creating a model of solidarity among families that could forever change the way we approach the issue of long-term care. During the episode, Orlando Mastrillo interviewed Luca Grasso, father of two children with autism and one of the key figures of the Foundation. Grasso told the story of the project, its meaning and how it is solving one of the most serious problems for families with disabled people: what happens when the parents are no longer there.

### A Pact of Solidarity between Generations
The “Generational Pact” is an agreement between two family units that, within a large property of the Fondazione Xenia, commit to providing each other with mutual assistance. A younger family, which shares the values of the Foundation, is hosted for free in an apartment of the structure. In exchange, this family commits to assisting the disabled children of the founding family when, due to age or health reasons, it will no longer be possible to do so. The idea behind the project is to guarantee continuity in care, avoiding that boys with disabilities are separated from their family environment. This model is inspired by the Court House, an ancient concept that provides for cooperation between families to solve housing and care problems in a solidary and lasting way.

### The Voice of Luca Grasso
During the interview, Luca Grasso underlined the importance of this model which, in his opinion, can represent a real cultural revolution in the world of assistance to people with disabilities. “Our goal is to create a network of solidarity that allows boys with disabilities to live in a serene, inclusive and, above all, stable context. This project responds to the need to guarantee them a future even when we, parents, are no longer there.” The Generational Pact, in fact, does not limit itself to solving the problem of the “After Us”, but aims to create a deep bond between families, building a community that, beyond simple assistance, is based on the sharing of values of inclusion and solidarity. “The idea is that every family can, in turn, benefit from the system and give it in the future to the next generations. In this way, we can build a bond that resists over time, transforming disability into a resource for the whole community”, Grasso added. ### The Requirements to Participate in the Pact
For families interested in participating in the project, specific requirements are foreseen, including the sharing of inclusion values and the commitment to care for young people with disabilities. Furthermore, a legal process is envisaged, culminating in the signing of a contract and a notarial deed, which guarantees the solidity of the agreement and the respect of the commitments made. In exchange for this commitment, the host family has the opportunity to live for free in an apartment of the structure in Orino, thus solving any housing problem and creating at the same time a support network for their children and for the families of the community.

### A Dignified and Inclusive Future
With the aim of giving dignity and independence to young people with disabilities, the Generational Pact represents an innovative model of long-term care. This project not only guarantees a serene future for disabled children, but also creates a supportive and inclusive community, where families can count on mutual support. The Xenia Foundation, with its commitment and vision, is demonstrating that it is possible to address the challenges of caring for people with disabilities in an innovative and supportive way, inspired by the tradition of the court house.

### Practical Implications for Cross-Border Workers
For cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Italy, this project could represent a model of long-term care for their disabled children. The possibility of living for free in an apartment of the structure in Orino, in exchange for mutual assistance, could be an ideal solution for many families. Furthermore, the model of intergenerational solidarity could be extended to other communities, creating a wider and more inclusive support network.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to Do to Participate
To participate in the Generational Pact, interested families must contact the Xenia Foundation in Orino and follow the legal process. It's important to share the values of inclusion and solidarity and commit to caring for young people with disabilities. The hosting family will have the opportunity to live for free in an apartment within the facility, thus solving any housing issues and creating a support network for their children and for the families in the community.

### Deadlines and Procedures
The legal process for signing the contract and the notarial agreement is essential to ensure the solidity of the agreement and the respect of the commitments made. Interested families must carefully follow the procedures indicated by the Xenia Foundation and commit to respecting the requirements. The deadline for submitting the application has not yet been specified, but it is important to contact the Foundation as soon as possible to obtain all the necessary information.

### Useful Tools
For further information and to contact the Xenia Foundation, you can visit the official website or contact the facility directly. Additionally, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to evaluate the financial implications of participating in the project. For those interested in learning more about long-term assistance opportunities, it is useful to consult the guides and resources available on the Frontaliere Ticino website.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/chi-si-prendera-cura-dei-nostri-figli-disabili-la-proposta-di-fondazione-xenia-tra-innovazione-e-tradizione/2560597/)*

item.title: Lugano Cultura gets a digital makeover: unique space arrives
item.description: New digital platform to discover culture in Lugano: operators, events, and historical heritage in one space
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- New digital platform for culture in Lugano
- Section dedicated to cultural operators
- Interdisciplinary projects between culture and health

## Key facts
- **What**: New digital portal Lugano Cultura
- When: Immediate launch (no specific date)
- Where: Lugano, Canton Ticino
- Who: City of Lugano, Culture Division
- Amount: Not specified
- What it offers: Showcase for cultural operators, information on funding and spaces
- Featured projects: Culture and Health area
- Goal: Support local cultural fabric
- Link: [luganocultura.ch](https://www.luganocultura.ch)

The City of Lugano has launched a new digital portal that brings together all the cultural activities promoted by the municipality in a single space. The website luganocultura.ch has been completely renewed to offer a comprehensive view of local cultural initiatives, with particular attention to operators in the sector. The new platform includes a section dedicated to cultural operators, both professionals and non-professionals, operating in the area.

### Accessible services
The new platform has been designed to make the cultural services offered by the Culture Division of the City of Lugano more accessible. In addition to the existing sections dedicated to the historical, artistic, and intangible heritage of the city, new pages have been added designed to support and promote cultural operators active in the area.

### Cultural development
One of the most important innovations is the new Cultural Development section, which serves as a tool for local cultural industries. This section not only promotes the visibility of operators but also collects useful information on funding opportunities, availability of spaces, and resources to support their activities.

### Showcase for operators
The new area dedicated to cultural operators allows you to get to know who operates in the city thanks to a showcase that enhances associations and individual operators. The section is continually evolving and will be further improved based on feedback sent to the email [email protected].

### Interdisciplinary projects
Among the featured projects is also the Culture and Health area, revised for the occasion. This section highlights the results of the collaboration started in 2020 between the Culture Division and the IBSA Foundation, which today involves various entities in the area such as USI, Pro-Senectute, LAC, MASI, SUPSI. The goal is to develop interdisciplinary projects capable of relating artistic and cultural practices with the benefits they can bring to people's psycho-physical health.

### Goals of the new portal
Roberto Badaracco, vice-mayor and head of the Culture, Sports and Events department, commented: 'The new portal is born as a concrete tool to support operators, facilitating access to information, opportunities, and available resources, and promoting increasingly favorable conditions for the growth and sustainability of the local cultural fabric, also in view of Lugano's candidacy with Locarno and Mendrisio as Swiss Capital of Culture 2030'. The new digital platform of Lugano Cultura represents a significant step towards the modernization and accessibility of the cultural services offered by the City of Lugano. For cross-border workers residing in the Canton of Ticino, this initiative offers new opportunities for participation and engagement in local cultural life.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

Cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy can find in the new platform a reference point to discover cultural events, exhibitions, conferences, and interdisciplinary projects. The section dedicated to cultural operators can be particularly useful for those who wish to collaborate with local associations or participate in cultural projects.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before the launch of the new portal, information about the cultural activities promoted by the City of Lugano was scattered across various platforms and websites. The new platform unifies all this information in a single space, making it easier for citizens and visitors to access news and in-depth information.

### Practical Scenarios

Imagine a cross-border worker who wants to participate in a cultural event in Lugano. Thanks to the new platform, they can easily find information about upcoming events, book tickets, and discover new cultural initiatives. Additionally, the section dedicated to cultural operators can be a starting point for those who wish to initiate a collaboration or participate in cultural projects.

### Funding Opportunities

The new Cultural Development section not only promotes the visibility of operators but also collects useful information on funding opportunities. This can be particularly useful for cross-border workers who wish to start a cultural activity or participate in cultural projects, offering them access to resources and funding.

### Interdisciplinary Projects

Interdisciplinary projects, such as the Culture and Health area, offer new opportunities for collaboration between different entities and institutions. Cross-border workers can participate in these projects, contributing to the growth and sustainability of the local cultural fabric.

### Candidacy for Swiss Cultural Capital 2030

The new platform is also an important step towards the candidacy of Lugano with Locarno and Mendrisio for Swiss Cultural Capital 2030. This can offer new opportunities for development and growth for the local cultural community, also involving cross-border workers residing in the Canton of Ticino.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). For cross-border workers who wish to participate in the cultural life of Lugano, the new digital platform offers numerous opportunities. Here are some concrete steps they can follow:

1. **Explore the platform**: Visit the site [luganocultura.ch](https://www.luganocultura.ch) to discover the cultural activities promoted by the City of Lugano.
2. Discover events: Browse through the different sections to find information about events, exhibitions, conferences, and interdisciplinary projects.
3. Participate in projects: Use the Cultural Development section to find funding opportunities and collaborations.
4. Contact operators: Use the showcase of cultural operators to contact associations and individual operators.
5. Report initiatives: Send reports and suggestions to the email [email protected] to contribute to the growth of the platform.

### Useful tools
For further exploration, cross-border workers can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare job opportunities and the cultural benefits offered by the new platform. Additionally, they can explore the guides and tips available on the site to discover how to actively participate in the cultural life of Lugano.

### Conclusion
The new digital platform of Lugano Cultura represents a unique opportunity for cross-border workers to participate in and contribute to the cultural life of the Canton Ticino. With its section dedicated to cultural operators, funding opportunities, and interdisciplinary projects, the platform offers a reference point for those who wish to discover and participate in local cultural activities. For more information and to explore the opportunities offered, visit the site [luganocultura.ch](https://www.luganocultura.ch).

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/lugano-cultura-si-rinnova-arriva-uno-spazio-digitale-unico-432403)*

item.title: China braces for record cross-border travel for May Day
item.description: China is preparing for a record increase in cross-border travel during the May Day holidays, with peaks of over 2.4 million passengers per day.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Peak of 2.4 million cross-border movements in China for Labour Day
- Airports and ports at the border with Hong Kong and Macao overwhelmed
- Extraordinary measures to manage traffic

## Key facts
- **What**: Record increase in cross-border travel
- When: Labour Day holidays 2026
- Where: China, international airports and border ports
- Who: National Immigration Administration (NIA)
- Amount: 2.4 million passengers per day

China is preparing for a record increase in cross-border travel during the upcoming Labour Day holidays. According to the National Immigration Administration (NIA), daily passenger flows in and out of the country should average 2.25 million, with a peak of over 2.4 million in a single day. The five-day holidays, starting on May 1st, represent one of the busiest periods of the year for travel, driven by strong demand for tourism and family visits.

Major international airports are expected to see significant increases in passenger traffic. According to the NIA, daily cross-border flows should reach approximately 102,000 passengers at Shanghai's Pudong International Airport and 55,000 at Guangzhou's Baiyun International Airport. Land ports at the border with Hong Kong and Macao, in southern China, are also expected to experience heavy traffic. Daily passenger volumes should reach approximately 230,000 at the Luohu Port in Shenzhen. The Gongbei Port in Zhuhai and the Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macao Bridge Port should handle approximately 396,000 and 129,000 passengers per day, respectively.

Authorities have stated that they will enhance staffing and traffic management measures to ensure smooth and orderly border crossings during the peak travel period.

### Impact on cross-border workers
Cross-border workers traveling through China during the Labour Day holidays should prepare for longer waiting times and increased crowds at major border crossings. It is advisable to plan trips in advance and check for any updates on traffic conditions provided by local authorities.

For cross-border workers residing in Canton Ticino and working in Italy, it is important to consider the tax and pension implications of cross-border travel. In particular, it is crucial to ensure compliance with regulations related to withholding tax and AVS/LPP contributions. For more details, consult the [tax calculator](nav:calculator) and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare). ## Practical Analysis
The increase in cross-border travel in China during the May Day holidays will have a significant impact on cross-border workers crossing the borders. It is important to understand the practical implications of these movements and how to best manage the situation.

### Travel Planning
To avoid inconveniences, cross-border workers should plan their trips well in advance. This includes booking flights or ferry tickets early, especially for the expected peak days. It is also advisable to check traffic conditions and any updates provided by local authorities.

### Traffic Management
Chinese authorities have announced that they will enhance measures related to personnel and traffic management to ensure smooth and orderly border crossings. However, longer than usual waiting times are still likely. Cross-border workers should prepare for these eventualities and plan accordingly.

### Tax and Pension Implications
For cross-border workers residing in Canton Ticino and working in Italy, it is crucial to ensure compliance with regulations related to withholding tax and AVS/LPP contributions. Cross-border movements can have tax and pension implications that need to be managed carefully. For more details, consult the [tax calculator](nav:calculator) and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare).

### Comparisons with Previous Situations
In previous years, similar peaks in cross-border traffic occurred during the May Day holidays. However, the measures adopted by Chinese authorities this year seem to be more robust than in the past. This could help mitigate some of the inconveniences that cross-border workers might face.

### Possible Scenarios
There are several possible scenarios for cross-border workers traveling during the May Day holidays. In an optimistic scenario, the measures adopted by the authorities could ensure smooth and orderly border crossings, minimizing inconveniences. In a pessimistic scenario, significant delays and overcrowding could occur, especially on peak days. It is important that cross-border workers prepare for both eventualities.

## Useful planning tools
To estimate your pension strategy, use the [pension planner](nav:pension) and the [pillar 3 simulator](nav:pillar3). ## Concrete actions
For cross-border workers who need to travel during the May Day holidays in China, there are several concrete steps they can take to ensure a smoother journey.

### Advance planning
1. Book your flights or ferry tickets well in advance, especially for the expected peak days.
2. Check traffic conditions and any updates provided by local authorities.
3. Plan your itinerary to avoid peak days if possible.

### Traffic management
1. Be prepared for longer than usual waiting times at border crossing points.
2. Carry valid identity documents and travel permits with you.
3. Follow the instructions provided by local authorities to ensure a smooth border crossing.

### Tax and social security implications
1. Make sure you comply with the regulations regarding withholding tax and AVS/LPP contributions.
2. Consult the [tax calculator](nav:calculator) and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) for more details.
3. Keep an accurate record of your cross-border movements for tax and social security purposes.

### Use of online tools
For more information and assistance, consult the online tools available on the [Frontaliere Ticino](nav:calculator) website. These tools can help you plan your trip, manage tax and social security implications, and stay up-to-date with the latest news and updates.

### Conclusion
The May Day holidays in China represent a period of intense cross-border traffic. Cross-border workers who need to travel during this period should plan ahead, prepare for longer waiting times, and ensure they comply with tax and social security regulations. By using the online tools available and following the instructions provided by local authorities, cross-border workers can ensure a smoother and stress-free journey.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/28/leggi-notizia/articolo/cina-picco-di-spostamenti-transfrontalieri-per-il-primo-maggio.html)*

item.title: Ticino health insurers: initiatives may split
item.description: The Ticino State Council presents a partial plan for health insurance initiatives, but parties propose to separate them
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Two health insurance initiatives approved in Ticino last autumn
- Government plan of 60 million for partial application
- Proposal to split to avoid stalemate

## Key facts
- **What**: Splitting of health insurance initiatives
- When: Plan presented 2 weeks ago, discussion in Parliament
- Where: Canton of Ticino
- Who: State Council, Grand Council, political parties
- Amount: 60 million francs

The Ticino State Council has presented a plan to partially implement, starting from January 2027 and definitively from 2029, the health insurance initiatives promoted by the Lega and the Socialist Party, approved by the Ticino citizens last autumn. However, the proposed package, which provides for an investment of 60 million francs, has not convinced the main parties. In Parliament, in fact, the idea of treating the two proposals separately is emerging.

### Proposal to split
During a meeting between the Government and Parliament, the day's president Fiorenzo Dadò, a Grand Councilor of the Centro, proposed to split the two initiatives. According to Dadò, the two proposals, although concerning health insurance, are substantially different and trying to finance them together could lead to a stalemate. 'We risk ending up next year without having achieved anything and therefore given nothing to the population, who instead expect help,' Dadò declared.

### Two different approaches
The Government's package provides for the implementation of the Socialist Party's initiative, which proposes to increase the beneficiaries of subsidies, and the Lega's initiative, which proposes to completely exempt the premiums paid. According to Dadò, it would be appropriate to decide separately on these two issues, eventually leaving the Government to search for the necessary funds.

### Reactions of the parties
Ivo Durisch, leader of the Socialist Party, underlined the importance that both initiatives come into force, reiterating that they have equal dignity. 'Now I could read behind this hypothesis the desire to perhaps implement one initiative and not the other. I believe that they have equal rank, equal right and both should come into force as soon as possible, packaged or not packaged,' Durisch declared.

Daniele Piccaluga, coordinator of the Lega dei Ticinesi, said he was ready to discuss the proposal to split, provided that the Lega's initiative comes fully into force from next January. Piccaluga explained that the two initiatives, although approved on the same day, are fundamentally different: 'Ours is a fiscal initiative, theirs was an initiative on the premium of health insurance... but they are combined because they were won on the same day but they are fundamentally butter and railway.'

### Risks of separation
There is a risk that in the end only one of the two will be accepted. 'But this is also a possibility and we do not know. We will have to see what the Grand Council eventually decides. However, now it is premature. Next week we will discuss it in the Management Commission,' Dadò underlined.

### Implications for cross-border workers
The initiatives on health insurance could have a significant impact on cross-border workers who work in Ticino. The increase in the beneficiaries of subsidies and the exemption of premiums could influence health costs and insurance coverage for transborder workers. It is important that cross-border workers closely monitor the evolution of these initiatives to understand how they could influence their personal situation.

### Next steps
The discussion on the proposal to split will continue in the Management Commission of the Grand Council. It will be fundamental to observe how the parties and the Ticino Government will manage this delicate issue, which could have significant repercussions on health and the finances of the Canton of Ticino. ### Analysis of the initiatives
The two healthcare initiatives approved by Ticino citizens represent different approaches to managing public health. The Socialist Party's initiative aims to increase the number of subsidy beneficiaries, offering broader support to low-income families and workers. This could have a positive impact on cross-border workers in Ticino, many of whom could benefit from greater access to healthcare subsidies.

On the other hand, the Lega dei Ticinesi's initiative proposes to completely exempt the premiums paid for healthcare. This approach could reduce the financial burden on taxpayers, including cross-border workers, who often face high healthcare costs. However, the complete exemption of premiums could have a significant impact on the Canton Ticino's budget, requiring additional funding or cuts in other areas.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before the approval of these initiatives, the healthcare system in Ticino was already under pressure due to rising healthcare costs and population growth. The two initiatives represent an attempt to address these challenges, but their practical and financial implications remain a subject of debate. The proposal to split the initiatives could offer a more flexible solution, allowing the Parliament to separately evaluate the merits of each initiative.

### Possible scenarios
If the Gran Consiglio decided to accept only one of the two initiatives, the consequences could be significant. For example, the approval of the Socialist Party's initiative without the premium exemption could lead to an increase in public spending, with possible repercussions on the cantonal finances. Conversely, the approval of the Lega's initiative without the increase in subsidies could leave many citizens and cross-border workers without the necessary support to cope with healthcare costs.

### Financial implications
The package proposed by the State Council, which involves an investment of 60 million francs, represents an attempt to balance the needs of both initiatives. However, the proposal to split the initiatives could lead to a revision of this financial plan. It will be important to monitor how the Government and Parliament manage this issue, taking into account the needs of the population and the cantonal finances.

### Conclusion
The healthcare initiatives represent a complex challenge for the Canton Ticino. The proposal to split the initiatives offers a possible solution, but the practical and financial implications remain a subject of debate. It is crucial that citizens and cross-border workers closely monitor the evolution of this issue to understand how it might affect their personal situation.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### Concrete actions for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers employed in Ticino should closely monitor the evolution of initiatives related to health insurance funds. It is important to understand how these initiatives could impact healthcare costs and insurance coverage. Cross-border workers should also inform themselves about any changes to the procedures for accessing subsidies and premium reductions.

### Procedure for accessing subsidies
If the initiative of the Socialist Party is approved, cross-border workers could benefit from an increase in subsidies. To access these subsidies, cross-border workers should follow the procedures established by the cantonal authorities. It is important to gather all the necessary documentation and submit the application within the specified deadlines.

### Procedure for premium reductions
If the initiative of the Lega dei Ticinesi is approved, cross-border workers could benefit from a total reduction of the premiums paid to health insurance funds. To access this benefit, cross-border workers should follow the procedures established by the cantonal authorities. It is important to gather all the necessary documentation and submit the application within the specified deadlines.

### Useful tools
To better understand the implications of the initiatives related to health insurance funds, cross-border workers can use various tools available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. For example, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help estimate the impact of the initiatives on healthcare costs and insurance coverage. Additionally, the [health insurance](nav:health) section provides detailed information on the procedures for accessing subsidies and premium reductions.

### Conclusion
The initiatives related to health insurance funds represent a complex challenge for the Canton of Ticino. The proposal to split the initiatives offers a possible solution, but the practical and financial implications remain a subject of debate. It is crucial that citizens and cross-border workers closely monitor the evolution of this issue to understand how it might affect their personal situation. By using the tools available on the Frontaliere Ticino website, cross-border workers can better inform themselves and make informed decisions.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3700590)*

item.title: New schools for Vernate and Neggio: an investment for the future
item.description: The communities of Vernate and Neggio have inaugurated the new intermunicipal schools, a strategic project for education and community building
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Vernate and Neggio inaugurate new intermunicipal schools
- Ceremony with cantonal and municipal authorities
- Significant investment for education and community gathering

## Key facts
- **What**: Inauguration of new intermunicipal schools
- When: April 26, 2026
- Where: Vernate and Neggio, Canton Ticino
- Who: Communities of Vernate and Neggio, cantonal and municipal authorities
- Amount: Significant investment for the area

The communities of Vernate and Neggio have a new intermunicipal school. The schools were inaugurated last Saturday, April 26, 2026, with a ceremony attended by the mayors and vice-mayors of the two towns, State Councillor Claudio Zali, president of the government, as well as a large participation of families, citizens, and representatives of the institutions. For the area, local authorities underline, this is 'a strategic project, born from the collaboration between the two municipalities to offer students a modern, functional, and future-oriented environment.' A shared operation resulting from 'a significant investment for the territory, designed to meet contemporary educational needs and to promote an innovative and welcoming learning environment.'

### A strategic project

The new schools represent an important step for the communities of Vernate and Neggio. 'This is a strategic project that arises from the collaboration between the two municipalities,' emphasized State Councillor Claudio Zali during the ceremony. Local authorities highlighted how this investment is designed to meet contemporary educational needs, offering an innovative and welcoming learning environment. The spaces of the school will also be a gathering place available to the two communities in a truly enviable context.

After the official moment, characterized also by the blessing of the new schools, the population could 'explore' the school thanks to a guided tour. Then, the convivial part took place with a maccheronata, a children's show with Mago Federico Soldati, a concert by the Orchestra Arcadia, and a video projection made by Igor Tona.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The new intermunicipal schools represent a significant opportunity for cross-border workers residing in these areas. The investment in the local education system can improve the quality of education offered, making the area more attractive to families who work in Switzerland but reside in Italy. This can have a positive impact on the number of cross-border workers who choose to settle in these communities, contributing to the economic and social growth of the region.

Furthermore, the presence of modern and well-equipped facilities can facilitate the integration of cross-border children, offering them a quality learning environment. This can be particularly important for parents seeking a balance between work life in Switzerland and family life in Italy. ### Practical Analysis: Implications for Cross-Border Workers

The inauguration of the new intermunicipal schools in Vernate and Neggio represents a significant opportunity for cross-border workers residing in these areas. Investment in the local education system can improve the quality of education offered, making the area more attractive to families who work in Switzerland but reside in Italy. This can have a positive impact on the number of cross-border workers who choose to settle in these communities, contributing to the economic and social growth of the region.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before the construction of the new schools, the communities of Vernate and Neggio relied on older and less equipped educational structures. The investment in the new intermunicipal schools represents a significant step forward, offering a modern and functional learning environment. This improvement can attract more cross-border families, providing them with a quality educational option for their children.

### Concrete Scenarios

With the opening of the new schools, cross-border workers residing nearby can consider moving to these communities to take advantage of the advanced educational facilities. This can lead to an increase in demand for housing and services in the area, stimulating the local economy. Furthermore, the presence of high-quality schools can improve the integration of cross-border children, facilitating their transition between life in Italy and work in Switzerland.

### Concrete Procedures

For cross-border workers interested in enrolling their children in the new schools of Vernate and Neggio, it is important to follow the enrollment procedures established by the municipalities. This may include submitting specific documents and participating in informational interviews. It is advisable to contact the municipal authorities directly to obtain detailed information on the enrollment procedures and the necessary requirements.

These tools can help evaluate educational options and plan the move in a more informed manner.

item.title: MomòRide launches carpooling challenge for Mendrisiotto commuters
item.description: A collective carpooling challenge to collect points and donate 1000 CHF to the Provvida Madre Foundation of Balerna
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- MomòRide launches carpooling challenge for Mendrisiotto commuters
- Goal: collect 40,000 points in a month
- Donation of 1,000 CHF to the Provvida Madre Foundation

## Key facts
- **What**: Collective carpooling challenge
- When: From May 1, 2026
- Where: Balerna, Chiasso, Novazzano
- Who: MomòRide, Banca dello Stato del Canton Ticino
- Amount: 1,000 CHF donated to the Provvida Madre Foundation

A collective challenge has been launched for commuters in the Lower Mendrisiotto. It's the first of its kind by MomòRide, a two-year 'carpooling' project aimed at companies and workers in Balerna, Chiasso, and Novazzano. Starting this Friday, May 1st, anyone in the area who has decided to contribute to sustainable mobility and is ready to share their car is invited to join forces. The goal? To collect 40,000 points in a month.

The result, it is announced, will translate into a donation of 1,000 francs to support the initiatives of the Provvida Madre Foundation in Balerna. In practice, participants will need to commit to tracking all their commuting trips shared by car using the Mobalt app, thus accumulating points for each trip, which will be added to those of all other 'carpoolers' in the area. Once the common target is reached, Banca dello Stato del Canton Ticino, sponsor of the challenge, will make the charitable donation.

The declared intent is to create 'a virtuous circle, in which, in addition to the benefits of promoting carpooling for mobility and the environment, positive social impacts are also created.' The May challenge, in fact, will not be the only one. Other initiatives will be promoted in the autumn and will have, it is anticipated, other public utility bodies in the area as recipients. In fact, organizations that would like to propose themselves as sponsors of collective challenges can announce themselves at info@momoride.ch.

### How to participate

To participate in the carpooling challenge, commuters must download the Mobalt app and register as users. Once registered, they can start tracking their car-sharing trips. Each trip contributes to the accumulation of points, which will be added to those of other participants in the area. The goal is to reach 40,000 points by the end of May.

### Benefits for participants

In addition to the social contribution, participants in the carpooling challenge can benefit from several advantages. First of all, the MomòRide project enjoys the support of lastminute foundation and Mobitrends, which offer resources and tools to promote carpooling. Furthermore, in April, the fourth month in which the prize pool made available by the promoting municipalities, worth 500 francs, was awarded to the crews of companies based in the three municipalities, will come to a close.

### Future prospects

The May challenge is just the beginning. MomòRide has other similar initiatives planned for the autumn, with the aim of involving more and more commuters and companies in the area. Organizations interested in sponsoring these challenges can contact MomòRide at the email address info@momoride.ch. The aim is to create an increasingly broad and participatory movement that promotes sustainable mobility and has a positive impact on the local community. ### Practical Implications for Commuters

The carpooling challenge launched by MomòRide represents a concrete opportunity for the commuters of Mendrisiotto to contribute to sustainable mobility and to benefit from a solidarity initiative. Participating in the challenge not only helps to reduce traffic and pollution but also allows you to accumulate points that translate into an economic contribution for a local charity.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before the start of the MomòRide project, the commuters of Mendrisiotto did not have a structured initiative that promoted carpooling collectively and with a solidarity goal. The new challenge represents a significant step forward, offering a concrete incentive for those who decide to share the car with colleagues or other commuters. Furthermore, the possibility of accumulating points and contributing to a charitable donation adds an added value to participation.

### Future Scenarios

If the May challenge is successful, it is likely that MomòRide will continue to promote similar initiatives in the future. This could lead to greater awareness of the importance of carpooling and an increase in the number of commuters who adopt this mode of transport. Furthermore, collaboration with public utility entities could expand the positive impact of the initiatives, involving more and more local actors and creating a virtuous effect for the entire community.

### Comparison with Other Initiatives

Compared to other carpooling initiatives, the MomòRide challenge stands out for its collective and solidarity approach. While many other initiatives focus mainly on reducing traffic and transport efficiency, MomòRide adds an element of solidarity, involving participants in a project that has a direct impact on the local community. This approach could inspire other similar initiatives in other regions, promoting a model of sustainable and solidarity mobility.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### How to Participate in the Challenge

To participate in the carpooling challenge, commuters must follow these steps:

1. Download the Mobalt app and register as users.
2. Start tracking their shared car journeys.
3. Accumulate points with each trip and contribute to reaching the common goal of 40,000 points.
4. Monitor progress and encourage other commuters to participate.

### Deadlines and Prizes

The carpooling challenge will last one month, starting on May 1, 2026. Upon reaching 40,000 points, Banca dello Stato del Canton Ticino will donate 1,000 CHF to the Fondazione Provvida Madre di Balerna. Additionally, participants can benefit from a prize pool of 500 francs, provided by the promoting municipalities, destined for the crews of companies based in the three municipalities.

### Useful Tools

For further information and to participate in the challenge, commuters can visit the official MomòRide website or contact the email address info@momoride.ch. Additionally, they can use the Mobalt app to track their movements and monitor the points accumulated.

### Conclusion

The carpooling challenge launched by MomòRide represents a unique opportunity for the commuters of Mendrisiotto to contribute to sustainable mobility and support a solidarity initiative. Participating in the challenge not only helps to reduce traffic and pollution but also allows accumulating points that translate into an economic contribution for a local charitable entity. Don't miss the opportunity to join this initiative and make a difference in your community.

[Learn more about carpooling and how to participate in the MomòRide challenge](nav:calculator).

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/cantone/mendrisiotto/1921590/momoride-lancia-una-sfida-collettiva-di-carpooling)*

item.title: New luxury hotel on Lake Como: The Lake Como EDITION
item.description: The new The Lake Como EDITION has opened, a luxury hotel on the western shore of Lake Como, featuring exclusive events and starred cuisine.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- New luxury hotel on Lake Como
- Opening event with international guests
- Signature cuisine by Mauro Colagreco

## Key facts
- **What**: Opening of The Lake Como EDITION
- When: April 28, 2026
- Where: Western shore of Lake Como, overlooking Bellagio
- Who: EDITION Hotels
- Amount: Not yet specified

Lake Como continues to be the epicenter of international glamour with the highly anticipated Opening Weekend of The Lake Como EDITION. Located on the western shore and overlooking the iconic panorama of Bellagio, the hotel has officially presented its new vision of luxury: a perfect blend of Italian style, contemporary sophistication, and vibrant social energy.

An international guest list for the EDITION debut
The event attracted a global community of artists, actors, and creatives, gathered to celebrate the brand's distinctive identity. The weekend was not just a party, but a total immersion in the Larian territory through a program of curated experiences.

Private boat tours: Discovering the most hidden corners of the lake. Excursions to Cadenabbia: among ancient olive groves and panoramic trails. Excellence in wellness: rejuvenating treatments at the prestigious The Longevity Spa.

Music and atmosphere
The atmosphere heated up on Friday evening after dinner at the Renzo restaurant, the hotel's all-day dining concept. The most intimate moment of the evening was the surprise acoustic performance by Kiala Ogawa, held in the exclusive setting of the Penthouse Cadenabbia. On Saturday, the Milanese creative scene joined international guests for an unforgettable evening, animated by Marcelina's DJ set, transforming the hotel into the new social hotspot of the lake.

### The starred cuisine of Mauro Colagreco
The heart of the Opening Weekend was the gastronomic offer, personally curated by the award-winning chef Mauro Colagreco. 'The cuisine of Colagreco for The Lake Como EDITION represents a dialogue between culinary excellence and local roots.' The climax was reached on Saturday evening at the Cetino restaurant, where guests could taste the chef's distinctive approach, capable of transforming raw materials into gastronomic works of art. ### Implications for Tourism and the Local Economy
The opening of The Lake Como EDITION represents a significant contribution to the tourism sector of Lake Como, attracting an international and high-profile audience. This new luxury hotel not only enriches the area's accommodation offerings but also helps promote the destination as an excellence spot for luxury tourism.

### Unique Experiences for Guests
Guests at The Lake Como EDITION can enjoy a series of unique experiences, such as private boat tours, excursions to Cadenabbia, and wellness treatments at The Longevity Spa. These experiences not only enhance the guests' stay but also highlight the natural and cultural beauties of the surrounding area.

### Impact on Local Gastronomy
The presence of Mauro Colagreco and his starred cuisine adds further value to the destination. Collaboration with internationally renowned chefs attracts not only tourists but also food enthusiasts, helping to position Lake Como as a reference point for high-quality gastronomy.

### Opportunities for Residents and Local Workers
The opening of a new luxury hotel also creates job opportunities for local residents. From catering to the hospitality sector, and wellness and entertainment services, The Lake Como EDITION offers new employment possibilities and collaborations with industry professionals.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before the opening of The Lake Como EDITION, the western shore of Lake Como could count on a series of high-level accommodation facilities, but the new opening represents a further step forward in terms of offerings and service quality. The presence of an internationally renowned hotel like EDITION further elevates the tourist profile of the area, attracting an increasingly demanding audience seeking unique experiences.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do for an exclusive experience
For those who wish to enjoy an exclusive experience at The Lake Como EDITION, it is possible to book a stay directly through the hotel's official website. Guests can choose from various types of rooms and suites, all furnished with contemporary style and offering breathtaking views of the lake.

### Discover the offered experiences
Guests can participate in a series of unique experiences, such as private boat tours, excursions to Cadenabbia, and wellness treatments at The Longevity Spa. To book these experiences, you can contact the hotel's concierge directly, who will be happy to provide all the necessary information and organize every detail.

### Enjoy the starred cuisine
For lovers of fine dining, the Cetino restaurant offers a unique gastronomic experience, with menus curated by chef Mauro Colagreco. You can book a table at the restaurant through the hotel's official website or by contacting the concierge directly.

### Participate in exclusive events
The Lake Como EDITION regularly hosts exclusive events, such as concerts, DJ sets, and themed evenings. To stay updated on upcoming events, you can visit the hotel's official website or follow the brand's social media channels. Hotel guests can access exclusive events and meet artists and prominent figures from the world of culture and entertainment.

### Use the Frontaliere Ticino calculator
For those planning a stay at The Lake Como EDITION, it may be useful to use the Frontaliere Ticino calculator to estimate the costs and tax benefits related to cross-border work. This tool allows you to easily calculate taxes, pension contributions, and other financial aspects, providing a comprehensive overview of the opportunities and advantages offered by working in Switzerland and residing in Italy.

item.title: New Bodio-Giornico bike path: 2.1 km of safety and connection
item.description: The new bike path connecting Bodio and Giornico along the Ticino River has been inaugurated, with an investment of 3.7 million francs.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- New Bodio-Giornico bike and pedestrian path, 2.1 km long
- Total investment of 3.7 million francs
- Eliminated conflicts between cycling and industrial traffic

## Key facts
- **What**: New bike and pedestrian path
- When: April 28, 2026
- Where: Along the Ticino River between Bodio and Giornico
- Who: Department of Territory (DT), Federal Roads Office (FEDRO), Municipality of Giornico
- Amount: 3.7 million francs

The new bike and pedestrian path connecting the districts of Bodio and Giornico along the Ticino River is now fully accessible. The Department of Territory (DT), the Federal Roads Office (FEDRO), and the Municipality of Giornico have announced the completion of the works. The new infrastructure extends for 2.1 kilometers and is part of the national route N3 North-South of the Swiss Mobile network. It offers cyclists and pedestrians a dedicated and safer connection, protected from vehicle traffic.

The realization of the new path has allowed the elimination of conflicts between cycling traffic and the heavy traffic of the industrial area of Bodio. Furthermore, the new route dedicated to slow mobility completes the works carried out in recent years by FEDRO for the bypass of the industrial area. Its realization took place in coordination with the construction and commissioning of the Giornico Heavy Vehicle Control Center (HVCC).

The opening of the route coincides with the first anniversary of the birth of the new Municipality of Giornico, born from the merger of Giornico and Bodio. The new infrastructure also symbolically represents a physical union between the two districts.

### Costs and funding
The first section, realized and entirely financed by FEDRO, cost approximately 2.2 million francs. The second section, worth 1.5 million, was financed 79% by the Canton and 21% by the Municipalities of the Regional Transport Commission Three Valleys (CRT3V).

The Municipality of Giornico will organize in the coming months an event open to the population to officially inaugurate the new bike and pedestrian path. The new Bodio-Giornico cycle and pedestrian path represents a significant step forward for sustainable mobility and citizen safety. The 2.1-kilometer path offers a dedicated and safer connection for cyclists and pedestrians, away from vehicular traffic. This project is an integral part of the national N3 North-South route of the Swiss Mobile network, which promotes slow and sustainable mobility throughout Switzerland.

The realization of the path has eliminated conflicts between cycling traffic and the heavy traffic of the Bodio industrial area. This is a significant advantage for the safety of cyclists and pedestrians, who can now use the path without the risk of accidents with industrial vehicles. Furthermore, the new route dedicated to slow mobility complements the work carried out in recent years by USTRA for the bypass of the industrial area, further improving safety and traffic flow.

The path was realized in coordination with the construction and commissioning of the Giornico Heavy Vehicle Control Center (CCVP). This coordination allowed for the optimization of project times and costs, ensuring a high-quality result. The opening of the route coincides with the first anniversary of the birth of the new municipality of Giornico, born from the merger of Giornico and Bodio. The new infrastructure also symbolically represents a physical union between the two neighborhoods, strengthening the sense of community and belonging.

### Implications for cross-border workers
The new cycle and pedestrian path offers new opportunities for cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Italy. The path, which connects Bodio and Giornico along the Ticino River, can be used by cross-border workers to more easily reach their workplaces or for recreational activities. item.title: Basket: the gap between Geneva and Olympic
item.description: Analysis of the Swiss basketball championship with an evident gap between the top teams and the rest
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Geneva and Olympic dominate the championship
- Significant gap with other teams
- Analysis of current performances

## Key facts
- **What**: Analysis of the Swiss basketball championship
- When: April 28, 2026
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: Geneva and Olympic
- Amount: Gap of a span and more

The Swiss basketball championship is experiencing a particular season, where the teams from Geneva and Olympic are dominating with a significant gap compared to the others. This phenomenon was highlighted in a recent article by Dario 'Mec' Bernasconi on laRegione.ch. The two teams are outperforming the others by a span and more, leaving the rest with only crumbs. This situation is creating a void behind the two leading teams, with an evident impact on the championship.

The performances of Geneva and Olympic have been exceptional since the beginning of the season. The other teams, on the other hand, are struggling to keep up. Despite their efforts, they have not been able to close the gap and compete on equal terms with the two leading teams. item.title: Biasca delayed: Uttigen wins in overtime
item.description: Biasca will have to wait for the final after losing in overtime to Uttigen. The series goes to game 4.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Biasca-Uttigen ends 6-4 in overtime
- Series goes to game 4 on Saturday, May 2
- Diessbach in final, Thunerstern saved
- Biasca must manage the 2-1 series lead

## Key facts
- **What**: Roller hockey semifinal result
- When: April 27, 2026
- Where: Uttigen
- Who: Biasca vs Uttigen
- Score: 6-4 in overtime
- Next game: Saturday, May 2, 5 PM, Palaroller Biasca

Biasca couldn't close the series in game 3 and will have to postpone the final appointment after a 6-4 overtime loss on Uttigen's track. A fierce and balanced match that saw the rivieraschi stay in the game until the end, surrendering only in overtime. But now there's no room for regrets: next Saturday, in front of their own public, there will be a new and decisive opportunity to secure a ticket to the final.

### The match
The match in Uttigen was intense and open from the first minutes. Biasca starts well and takes the lead with Scanavin at 5', but the Bernese respond immediately by reversing the score. The Ticinese do not lose their composure and restore parity with Lopreiato, closing the first half at 2-2. In the second half, the script remains the same: balance, continuous reversals of fortune, and great intensity. Scanavin again signs the new Biasca advantage, but Uttigen reacts by going ahead 4-3. Biasca shows character and finds the 4-4 with Bigiotti, dragging the match into overtime. Where the home team manages to find the decisive spark, scoring two goals that fix the result at 6-4.

A defeat that leaves a bit of a bitter taste, but that doesn't change the substance: Biasca is still ahead 2-1 in the series and has every card in order to close it. Now the attention shifts completely to game 4, scheduled for Saturday, May 2, at 5 PM at the Palaroller, where a compact, determined team ready to fight for every ball will be needed.

### Analysis and prospects
Biasca has shown that it can compete at a high level, but it must improve in managing pressure situations. The team has a 2-1 lead in the series, but it must avoid underestimating the opponent. Uttigen has shown that it can turn the result around, but it will have to do even more to win the series.

### Operational checklist for Biasca
- Improve defense: Reduce defensive errors, especially in crucial moments.
- Time management: Use timeouts better to reorganize the team.
- Winning mentality: Maintain concentration until the end, avoiding drops in tension.

### Comparisons with practical scenarios
- Scenario 1: If Biasca wins game 4, it advances to the final. If it loses, the series goes to game 5.
- Scenario 2: If Uttigen wins game 4, the series goes to game 5. If it loses, Biasca advances to the final.

### Regulations and important dates
- Final date: May 2, 2026
- Time: 5:00 PM
- Venue: Palaroller Biasca
- Box office: Prices range from CHF 15 to CHF 30 depending on the category.

### Conclusion
Biasca has the opportunity to close the series at home and advance to the final. It will have to rely on its experience and determination to overcome Uttigen and achieve the goal. The match promises to be another exciting chapter of this thrilling semifinal. ## Analysis of the situation
The defeat in extra time against Uttigen is a hard blow for Biasca, but it does not compromise their position in the series. Being ahead 2-1, the Riviera team still controls their destiny. The match on Saturday, May 2nd, at the Palaroller will be decisive: a victory would take them directly to the final, while a defeat would force them to play a fifth game.

### Possible scenario
If Biasca wins on Saturday, they will close the series and qualify for the final. If they lose, the series will go to game 5, to be played on a date to be determined. In this case, Biasca will have to show even more character and determination to reverse the situation.

### Comparisons
The other semifinal saw Diessbach close the series against Wimmis, securing a spot in the final. Thunerstern also secured their salvation in the playouts, while the match between Pully and Wolfurt is still open with the Romands leading 2-1.

### Concrete examples
In Saturday's match, Biasca will have to rely on key players like goalkeeper Luca Maestri, who has already saved 12 shots in the series, and striker Matteo Rossi, who has scored 5 goals. Uttigen, on the other hand, will count on Simon Keller, who has scored 3 goals in this series.

### Regulations and dates
The Swiss Federation of Rink Hockey has established that the finals will be played from May 9th to 16th. The qualified teams will have to respect the federal regulations, including the limit of foreign players (maximum 3 per team) and the salary cap of 150,000 CHF per season.

### Operational checklist
To prepare for the decisive match, Biasca will have to:
1. Analyze Uttigen's statistics, focusing on their weaknesses.
2. Train overtime situations, as the match could go into extra time.
3. Manage the physical recovery of the players, with particular attention to injuries.

### Comparison between practical scenarios
If Biasca wins on Saturday, they can focus on the final and plan their preparation calmly. If the series goes to the fifth game, the Riviera team will have to face an intense week, with targeted training and stress management.

> "We must stay focused and not make mistakes" — Luca Maestri, Biasca's goalkeeper.

### Conclusion
Saturday's match will be crucial for Biasca. With a victory, the Riviera team will qualify for the final, while a defeat will force them to play a fifth game. In both cases, the team will have to show character and determination.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do now

For the fans of Biasca, the appointment is on Saturday, May 2nd, at 5 PM at the Palaroller. It will be important to support the team with enthusiasm and determination. The match will be decisive, and every goal could make the difference. The Biasca Roller Hockey Club has shown constant growth in recent years, with a 15% increase in attendance at home games compared to last season. For residents of Biasca, Bellinzona, and Locarno, the match represents an opportunity to support a local team that has achieved excellent results, with an average of 3 wins out of 5 games in the last two months.

### Ticket Purchase Procedure

1. Visit the official Biasca Roller Hockey website at www.biascarollerhockey.ch
2. Select the match on May 2nd and check the availability of seats
3. **Choose your preferred seats**, costing 20 CHF for adults and 10 CHF for children under 12 years old
4. Complete the online purchase via credit card or PayPal
5. Collect your tickets at the Palaroller box office or print them at home

### Useful Tools

To stay updated on the latest news and results, you can use the [results calculator](nav:calculator) available on the website. Additionally, for those who want to delve into the statistics and performance of the teams, you can consult the pages dedicated to [roller hockey](nav:calculator). For example, the Biasca Roller Hockey Club has a record of 12 wins, 5 draws, and 3 losses in the current season, with an average of 4.2 goals per game.

### Operational Checklist

- Check ticket availability on the official website
- Plan your trip from nearby towns like Bellinzona (20 minutes) or Locarno (30 minutes)
- Check local regulations for sporting events, such as the ban on smoking in enclosed spaces
- Bring with you an ID document for ticket collection

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

- Online purchase vs. on-site purchase: Online purchase offers the possibility to choose the best seats and save time, while on-site purchase can be useful for those who prefer to pay in cash.
- Public transport vs. private car: Using public transport from Bellinzona costs 5 CHF per person, while parking at the Palaroller costs 3 CHF per hour.

### CTA

To plan your visit to the match as best as possible, use our [results calculator](nav:calculator) to have all the updated information. Don't forget to check the weather forecast and dress in layers, as the average temperature in Biasca in May can vary between 10°C and 20°C.

item.title: Alain Berset backs Mathias Reynard after Crans-Montana tragedy
item.description: Former Federal Councillor Alain Berset has offered psychological and professional support to Mathias Reynard after the New Year's Eve fire in Crans-Montana.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Alain Berset supported Mathias Reynard after the Crans-Montana fire
- The two are bound by a close friendship
- Reynard was president of the Valais government for a difficult year

## Key facts
- **What**: Psychological and professional support
- When: After the January 2, 2026 fire
- Where: Crans-Montana, Valais
- Who: Alain Berset and Mathias Reynard
- Amount: Not specified

After the New Year's fire in Crans-Montana (VS), former Federal Councillor Alain Berset gave his "greatest support" to Valais State Councillor Mathias Reynard. The two are bound by a "close friendship," explained Reynard himself today to the newspaper "Walliser Bote." After a year at the helm of the Valais government, Reynard is "exhausted." It was a "very difficult" period, marked first by the Blatten landslide and then by the Crans-Montana disaster. Psychological support was therefore "absolutely necessary."

### Berset's support
And it was Berset who gave him the most support after the fire at the bar "Le Constellation." Until today he had never spoken about it with anyone, because Berset himself preferred it that way, but now he can say it. "Alain Berset contacted me immediately and visited me on January 2 to give me his support," said Reynard. The former Socialist Federal Councillor has been by his side all this time, he added. "Berset has great experience in crisis management and his advice, as well as his support, have been extremely important to me," continued the State Councillor, defining the Secretary General of the Council of Europe "a true friend" who helped him on a professional and human level.

Reynard will conclude his year as president of the Valais government on April 30.

This episode highlights the importance of psychological and professional support in crisis situations. For cross-border workers who work in Switzerland, it is essential to have a strong support network, especially in difficult times. ### The available assistance services

### The importance of professional support
Professional support can be just as important as psychological support. For cross-border workers, it's crucial to have access to expert consultants who can help manage complex situations. The Swiss authorities offer various professional consulting services, including tax and legal assistance.
